text: You should apply in person to the Commissariat Général de la Police Judiciaire (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring: • Your original identity card • Two passport size photographs • A letter of introduction and explanation of why the police certificate is required You should apply to the Commissariat Général de la Police Judiciaire (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring: • Certified copy of your identity card • Certified copy of your passport • Two passport size photographs • A letter of introduction and explanation of why the police certificate is required You should apply in person to the Commissariat Général de la Police Judiciaire (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring: • Certified copy of your passport and proof of legal stay in Burundi • Two passport size photographs • A letter of introduction and explanation of why the police certificate is required You should apply to the Commissariat Général de la Police Judiciaire (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring: • Certified copy of your passport and proof of legal stay in Burundi • Two passport size photographs • A letter of introduction and explanation of why the police certificate is required All information and documents needed are explained above. Commissariat Général de la Police Judiciaire Service Central d’Identité Judiciaire B.P. 417, Bujumbura Telephone: (257) 22 57 83 Note : While applications by post will be accepted, if you are a non-resident citizen it is preferable to have a Burundi resident apply in person at Commissariat Général de la Police Judiciaire on your behalf.

heading: How to get a police certificate – Canada
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Canada
heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You don’t need to provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada. We’ll tell you if you need a Canadian criminal record check while your application is being processed.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Canada
heading: Special considerations
text: Fingerprints must be submitted electronically to Canadian Criminal Real Time Identification Services (CCRTIS). Electronic fingerprint submissions to the RCMP are not currently available from outside of Canada. You must contact an accredited company within Canada that will digitize ink fingerprints and submit them electronically to the RCMP. Paper submissions will not be processed and will be sent back to you. If you need to submit fingerprints for a citizenship application, they must be collected inside Canada. There’s currently a large demand for RCMP criminal record checks. Your request may be delayed. If this delay has affected you, we’ll automatically extend the deadline to provide the criminal record check by 30 days. You don’t need to contact us or the RCMP to extend the deadline.

You can also appoint a representative (a friend or relative) to apply at the Ministry of Justice or the High Courts on your behalf. Submit a request at the nearest embassy or consulate. • A proof of residence may be requested

Ask your local office how to apply. You can also nominate a representative to apply on your behalf. You can appoint a family member to apply for a police certificate on your behalf. They’ll need: • their original national ID card • your original national ID card • their original hukou booklet If their hukou registration has been cancelled, they will need a Certificate of Residence Cancellation from a local police station. • an application form for a Certificate of No Criminal Record • one (1) of the following: if they’re an immediate family member , a power of attorney from you • if they’re a non-immediate family member , a notarized power of attorney from a notary office The application process differs from city to city. Ask the local office in your household registration hukou booklet where you used to live how to apply. You may be able to apply: • on their website or mobile application • at the city’s local Public Security Bureau • at the local Public Security police station in the jurisdiction of the address in your household registration hukou booklet if your household registration is still valid. If your hukou…

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – China
heading: Special considerations
text: • You’ll need to provide either a Notarized or certified translation. • For Chinese citizens , please make sure the Certificate covers the period from birth to the current date . • A certificate with a QR code is recommended by the central government, however, certificates without the QR code are also acceptable. • If you are not a Chinese citizen , China will issue the “ Certificate of No Criminal Record ” only to “ Foreigner Residence Permit ” holders (currently or previously valid). It is important to present your foreigner residence permit along with your police clearance certificate request. • While some cities may support online applications for a police clearance certificate for foreigners , certain cities may still require foreigners to visit a Notary Public office first and obtain a Notarized Certificate of No Criminal Record through a Notary Public office. • If you are unable to provide a police certificate for a specific reason, or for a specific portion of your stay, this could delay processing for your application . Please upload a letter with the following statement: “I cannot provide a police certificate for the full time I spent in China because [insert reason].” The reason indicated will be reviewed, and a determination will be made on its relevance to the missing or required documents or information.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Colombia
heading: How to apply
text: Omnitempus (available in Spanish only) Email: servicios@omnitempus.com Phone: +57 (601) 611-0529, extension 214, 215 • how long the application will take • how much it will cost Do not apply for the Background Verification Certificate until you receive a file number from the Canadian visa office and instructions telling you to apply for the certificate. You’ll need to send a scanned copy of your ID card (Cédula de Ciudadanía o de Identidad) and a signed consent form (given by the service provider) authorizing them to send the Background Verification Certificate to the Canadian visa officer. Omnitempus will send us your Background Verification Certificate directly. Depending on the results of your background verification, you may be asked to provide additional information. Please submit the original confirmation email received from the service provider indicating that you have applied for a Background Verification certificate as part of your IRCC application.

You must bring: • Your original birth certificate • Your original passport • Your father’s full name and your mother’s maiden and married names • Place of residence in Comoros You must apply to the “ Tribunal de première instance ” of Moroni if you are from Grande Comoros (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring: • Copy of your birth certificate • Copy of your passport • Your father’s full name and your mother’s maiden and married names • Place of residence while in Comoros You must apply in person to the “ Tribunal de première instance ” of Moroni if you are from Grande Comoros (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring: • Your original birth certificate • Your father’s full name and your mother’s maiden and married names • Certified copy of passport and copies of visa/permits showing legal residence in Comoros • Place of residence in Comoros You must apply to the “ Tribunal de première instance ” of Moroni if you are from Grande Comoros (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring: • Copy of your birth certificate • Your father’s full name and your mother’s maiden and married names • Certified copy of passport and copies of visa/permits showing legal residence in Comoros • Place of residence while in Comoros All information and documents needed are explained above Greffier en Chef Tribunal de Moroni Comoros • For married women a marriage certificate must be provided • Non-residents may also apply in person to the Master of Justice, at the Comoros Embassy in their country of residence

heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for: • some temporary residence programs Check your application package or instructions for details.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Costa Rica
heading: How to apply
text: • online through the judicial website (available in Spanish only) You’ll be notified when your certificate is ready for pickup at the Judicial Register of the Supreme Court (check Contact information for the complete address). • in person at the Oficina del Registro Judicial (check Contact information for the complete address) Someone in Costa Rica must pick up your certificate for you. To apply for a certificate, you must write a letter to your nearest Costa Rican consulate requesting a Certificación de Antecedentes Penales (marked “Para usos varios internacionales”) . You must include the following information in your letter: • your Costa Rican identity card number and a photocopy of both sides of your identity card If you’re not a Costa Rican citizen, include your passport number. • the amount of time you lived in Costa Rica • your place and date of birth • the full name of both parents • details about the person you’ve authorized to pick up your certificate. You must include their full name The consulate will send your letter to the Judicial Register of the Supreme Court in San José. The person you authorized can pick up your certificate there once it’s ready (check Contact information for the full address). Your certificate must be authenticated by both of the offices below for it to be valid in Canada. Your friend or family member must bring your certificate to the offices in person at the following addresses: • Authentication Office of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Costa Rica Between avenues 7 and 9 and streets 11 and 13, San José, Costa Rica Phone: 2223-7555, Ext. 136, 188 or 220 • Supreme Court of Costa Rica Between avenues 6 and 8 and streets 19 and 21, San José For more information, visit the Embassy of Costa Rica website . If you don’t have someone in…

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Denmark (including Faroe Islands and Greenland)

text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for: • some temporary residence programs Check your application package or instructions for details.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Denmark (including Faroe Islands and Greenland)
heading: How to apply
text: If you have no criminal record in Denmark, the certificate will be issued in English, German, French and Spanish. If you do have previous convictions or entries, the certificate of criminal record will be issued in Danish. You are responsible for having it translated to English or French. To issue the certificate, Danish authorities need • written and signed consent • a photocopy of your valid passport • a postal address to send the certificate to Please email all documents to mvjyl-pac-kr@politi.dk . The handling time is approximately 3 working days, plus the time it takes for the letter to reach its destination via regular postal service. Due to Danish law, your certificate of criminal record cannot be sent to you by email. The issuance of a certificate of criminal record is free of charge. If you have a NemID, you can request a certificate of criminal record online (note that the form is in Danish). Personal criminal record checks ( Privat straffeattest ) are not accepted for IRCC purposes.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Denmark (including Faroe Islands and Greenland)
heading: Contact information
text: Rigspolitichefen, Afdeling D Kriminalregisteret P.O. Box 93 Landlystvej 34-40 2650 Hvidovre Denmark Fax: +45 46 33 14 78 For more information, see the Politi website .

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Dominica
heading: How to get a police certificate - Dominica
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Dominica
heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada. You will also be requested to provide a police certificate to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) if you declared a criminal record on your application for an electronic travel authorization, work permit or study permit.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Dominica
heading: How to apply
text: You should apply in person with: • all previous names used • current and previous places of residence You may apply in person, or have someone apply for you, or apply by mail. The police certificate will be mailed to you or given in person. You should include, with your application, the items listed above, plus: • a full set of fingerprints taken by a recognized police authority; • a self-addressed envelope.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Dominica
heading: Contact information
text: Submit your application to the following address: Criminal Investigation Department Police Headquarters Roseau, Dominica Telephone: 767-448-2222

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Czech Republic

heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada. pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Czech Republic
heading: How to apply
text: You should apply at any local or municipal authority office, Czech point (available in Czech only) or directly at the Criminal Register office in Prague. If you have a recognized electronic signature , you can also apply directly online to the electronic mailroom of the Criminal Register (this service is only offered in the Czech Republic). You should apply at your nearest Czech embassy or consulate.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Czech Republic
heading: Special considerations
text: • An official application form is available at any local or municipal office • Original and valid personal identity document with a photograph (such as a national ID card, a passport or a foreigner’s residence permit) will be needed to process your request. • If a representative is applying on your behalf, he/she must have notarized power of attorney (including a sworn translation in Czech if not already in that language) • You must have your certificate translated into English or French.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Czech Republic
heading: Contact information
text: For more information on fees, processes or on how to obtain a recognized electronic signature (in order to apply online), please visit the Ministry of Justice of the Czech Republic website . Unless you apply directly at the Criminal Register office in Prague, the results will be returned to you by mail.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Equatorial Guinea
heading: How to get a Police Certificate:
text: You must apply at the Ministry of Justice in Equatorial Guinea for a "Certificado de Antecedentes Penales" at: Ministry of Justice, Religious Affairs & Penitentiary Institutions of Equatorial Guinea Street Address Director General De Seguridad Nacional Avenida De La Independencia Malabo You must apply for the "Certificado de Antecedentes Penales" at the closest embassy or consulate of Equatorial Guinea. You must apply in person for a "Certificado de Antecedentes Penales" at the Ministry of Justice, Religious Affairs & Penitentiary Institutions of Equatorial Guinea Street Address Director General De Seguridad Nacional Avenida De La Independencia Malabo You can apply at your country’s embassy or diplomatic mission in (or responsible for Equatorial Guinea). The embassy or diplomatic mission will have to send your application for a police certificate to the Ministry of Justice in Equatorial Guinea. If the embassy or consulate office are unable to do this, you have to provide this information in writing as part of your application to Canada. • Certificado de Antecedentes Penales

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - El Salvador
heading: How to get a police certificate - El Salvador
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - El Salvador
heading: Name of the documents you need
text: • Constancia de Antecedentes Penales (also known as Solvencia de Antecedentes Penales ) • Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales (also known as Solvencia de Antecedentes Policiales )

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - El Salvador
heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for: • some temporary residence programs Check your application package or instructions for details.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - El Salvador
heading: How to apply
text: Apply in person or through an authorized third party for a Constancia de Antecedentes Penales through the Dirección General de Centros Penales in San Salvador (see Contact information for the complete addresses) or apply online . Apply for a Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales online or in person through the Dirección General de la Policía Nacional Civil y de Readaptación (see Contact Information for the complete address). Online applications shall be finalized and sent via email within 5 business days, but there may be exceptions. Apply for a Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales online or in person through the Dirección General de la Policía Nacional Civil (see Contact Information for the complete address). Apply for the Antecedentes Penales online or in person or through an authorized third party for the Constancia de Antecedentes Penales at the Dirección General de Centros Penales y de Readaptación in San Salvador (see Contact Information for the complete address). Apply for a Constancia de Antecedentes Penales and Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales in person at your nearest consulate of El Salvador. Please note: In person services outside of El Salvador to obtain the Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales can take two to three months, as the consulate sends the request to Foreign Affairs of El Salvador by diplomatic bag, and they will forward it to the police station, where the background is consulted. When the Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales is processed, it will be returned using the same procedure.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - El Salvador
heading: Documents you need
text: El Salvador nationals and residents: • the original and a photocopy of your valid Documento Único de Identificación (DUI) or your valid passport • the original and a photocopy of your passport • two frontal pictures and two pictures of your right side • the proper completed form (available at the consulate) • a letter stating why you require a police certificate (i.e., for immigration purposes)

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - El Salvador
heading: Special considerations
text: The Unit of Police Background Check reserves the right to not issue the Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales to people who were found to have criminal records and/or have not cleared their legal situation. In the event of an inconvenience obtaining the Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales , you may dial the toll-free number +1 888 301 1130 of the virtual consulate. You may contact your nearest consulate of El Salvador as well.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - El Salvador
heading: Contact information
text: Dirección General de Centros Penales y de Readaptación (Constancia de Antecedentes Penales) • Oficina Central 19 Calle Poniente, #320 entre 3a y 5a Avenida Norte Colonia Guadalupe, Edificio Duarte Novoa San Salvador Telephone: 2235-4095 • Registro de Armas Autopista Norte, Edificio Extránsito del Registro de Armas San Salvador Telephone: 2235-1554 • Santa Ana 3a Avenida Sur, casa #19, entre 5a y 7a Calle Oriente Barrio Santa Cruz, cerca de Iglesia el Carmen Santa Ana Telephone: 2447-6342 • San Miguel 8a Calle Poniente #602, Barrio San Francisco San Miguel Telephone: 2661-0614 • Sucursal adicional en San Miguel 3a Calle Poniente pasaje Joaquin Cardenas poligono a Casa #9, Colonia Santa Gertrudis San Miguel Telephone: 2661-2698 Dirección General de la Policía Nacional Civil (Constancia de Antecedentes Policiales) • Centro San Salvador Telephone: 2529-2600 • Soyapango (San Salvador) Telephone: 2277-9020 • Flor Blanca (San Salvador) Telephone: 2511-0500 • Santa Ana Telephone: 2420-8666 • Centro de Gobierno de San Miguel Telephone: 2661-2443

pageTitle: How to Get a Police Certificate - Ethiopia
heading: How to Get a Police Certificate - Ethiopia
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application. Do I need to provide a Police Certificate with my Application for Permanent Residence? No. Citizenship and Immigration Canada will send you a letter requesting a police certificate after you send your Application for Permanent Residence. You must bring that letter when you apply for a Police Certificate. Name of document(s) to get: Certificate of Good Conduct

pageTitle: How to Get a Police Certificate - Ethiopia
heading: How to get a Police Certificate
text: You should apply in person at the Ethiopian Federal Police Commission Forensic Investigation Department (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must allow your fingerprints to be taken and you must also bring: • A copy of your national identity card • A copy of your passport • Two passport-size photographs • The letter from the Canadian visa office requesting the certificate You should apply by sending your documents to the Ethiopian Federal Police Commission Forensic Investigation Department (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You may also ask a representative to submit the request in person on your behalf (he or she must bring proof of legal representation). You must bring: • An original set of fingerprints taken by the police authority of where you live • A copy of your passport • Two passport-size photographs • The letter from the Canadian visa office requesting the certificate You should apply in person at the Ethiopian Federal Police Commission Forensic Investigation Department (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must allow your fingerprints to be taken and you must also bring: • A copy of your passport • Two passport-size photographs • Copies of visa/permits showing the period of legal residence in Ethiopia • The letter from the Canadian visa office requesting the certificate You should apply by sending your documents to the Ethiopian Federal Police Commission Forensic Investigation Department (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You may also ask a representative to submit the request in person on your behalf (he or she must bring proof of legal representation). You must bring: • An original set of fingerprints taken by the police authority where you live •…

pageTitle: How to Get a Police Certificate – Eritrea
heading: How to Get a Police Certificate – Eritrea
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

pageTitle: How to Get a Police Certificate – Eritrea
heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You don’t need to provide a Police Certificate with your application. We will send you a file number and a letter requesting a clearance certificate after you send your application for permanent residence, citizenship, or for some temporary resident programs. You must bring your file number and that letter when you apply for a clearance certificate.

pageTitle: How to Get a Police Certificate – Eritrea
heading: How to apply
text: You should apply in person to the Eritrea Police Headquarters (view Contact information for the complete address). • bring your Identity card or passport • allow fingerprints to be taken • bring 2 passport size photographs • bring the letter and file number from IRCC or the Canadian High Commission requesting the certificate • pay the applicable fees If you’re a foreign resident of Eritrea, you must also provide a copy of your passport and copies of visas or permits showing your period of legal residence in Eritrea • Police certificates are only available to foreign residents with a proper residence permit. You should designate a representative with power of attorney to make the application in Eritrea to the Eritrea Police Headquarters (view Contact information for the complete address) or you can apply through the nearest Eritrean Embassy. You (or your designate) must: • bring a copy of your Identity card • bring a copy of your passport • bring a full set of your fingerprints • bring the letter and file number from IRCC or the Canadian High Commission requesting the certificate • pay the applicable fees • Police certificates are only available to Eritrean citizens who have done national service • Police certificates will not be issued to Eritrean citizens who cannot provide evidence that they have paid a state imposed tax which is placed on all Eritrean citizens, regardless of country of residence

pageTitle: How to Get a Police Certificate – Eritrea
heading: Contact information
text: Eritrea Police Headquarters P.O. Box 1223 Asmara, Eritrea Telephone: (+291) 1 127 799

heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada. You will also be requested to provide a police certificate to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) if you declared a criminal record on your application for an electronic travel authorization, work permit or study permit.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Finland
heading: How to apply
text: If your criminal record does not have entries, it can be written in English, French, German or Spanish. If your record has entries, you need to have it translated to English or French. The Legal Register Centre does not translate extracts that have entries.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Finland
heading: Special considerations
text: If you are a citizen of Finland, you do not need to provide a police certificate from this country with your application. However, an IRCC officer may request one at a later date. You must provide police certificates for all other countries or territories where you have lived for a total of six months or more since your 18th birthday.

heading: Contact information

Box 157 FI - 13101 Hämeenlinna, Finland Telephone: +358 - (0)3 - 622 3770 (From abroad) Fax: +358 - (0)3 - 622 3783 (From abroad) Email: oikeusrekisterikeskus@om.fi Website: Police and Border Guard Board (available in English and Finnish)

text: • Certificate of Good Conduct ( Führungszeugnis ) or • European Certificate of good conduct ( Europäisches Führungszeugnis ) for citizens of other European Union member states

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Germany
heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Germany
heading: How to apply
text: By mail —send your application to Federal Office of Justice at Bundesamt für Justiz Referat IV 2 53094 Bonn Online —at the website of the Federal Office of Justice (in German only). You’ll need • a valid and recognized electronic signature ( elektronischen Personalausweis ) such as an electronic residency permit ( elektronischen Aufenthaltstitel ) • a card-reading machine ( Kartenlesegerät ) In person –at the Federal Office of Justice located at Bundesamt für Justiz Besucherservice Adenauerallee 99 - 103 53113 Bonn

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Germany
heading: Special considerations
text: For more information on fees, processing times, how to apply online, how to obtain an electronic signature or to download the forms, please go to the website of the Federal Office of Justice . Your police certificate will be mailed to your residential address (even if you live abroad). It cannot be sent to a third party. Police certificates issued before February 2019 are in German only. You must give us a certified English or French translation of your certificate. New German police certificates are now in German, English and French, and do not need to be translated. German law restricts who can request a police certificate on your behalf. If you apply from overseas, you must have your personal data and signature confirmed by a German diplomatic representative, a foreign authority or a notary, or you must provide a certified photocopy of an official document. If your certificate contains entries and you live abroad, the Federal Office of Justice will forward your police certificate to the German embassy or consulate where you live and you will be invited to come and look at the document in person. You must give your consent to allow the German embassy/consulate to send this document to the responsible Canadian visa office.

heading: How to get a police certificate – Ghana

heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada, and for some temporary residence programs (please see the document checklist and/or visa office requirements for details).

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Ghana
heading: How to apply
text: Apply in person at the CID Headquarters in Accra by contacting the Ghana Police . Your fingerprints will be taken when you apply. You can also appoint a representative (a friend or relative) to apply at the CID Headquarters in Accra on your behalf.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Ghana
heading: Information and documents you need
text: Residents (if you have lived in Ghana for 3 months or more) • a completed an application form (fingerprint check) Non-residents (if you lived in Ghana for 3 months or more, and no longer live in Ghana) • a completed application form (nominal vetting) • 2 passport-size photographs with a white background (if you apply through a representative, you only need 1 photo) • the data page of your passport • your parents’ full names • your complete current address and telephone number • the name of the last school you attended • your fingerprints are requested, to be taken by a competent police officer or security authority

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Ghana
heading: Special considerations
text: Certificates are only available to people who have lived in Ghana for 3 months or more.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Haiti

heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for: • some temporary residence programs Check your application package or instructions for details.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Haiti
heading: How to apply
text: Apply in person at the Direction centrale de la police judiciaire d'Haïti (DCPJ) in Port-au-Prince (check contact information for the complete address). You must submit the following documents • a letter from IRCC stating that you must get a Haitian police certificate • the receipt for the applicable fees that you paid at the Direction générale des impôts (available in French only) • a copy of the first page of your passport showing your name and passport number • a copy of your birth certificate ( Acte de naissance or Extrait des Archives Nationales ) • a copy of your national identity card • a copy of your marriage certificate if you’re married ( Acte de marriage ) • two copies of a recent identification (passport) photograph • two separate identification documents with your picture (e.g. passport, electoral card, etc.) • a copy of your fingerprints (will be taken at the DCPJ) A friend or family member in Haiti can apply on your behalf. They must go to the Direction centrale de la police judiciaire d'Haïti (DCPJ) in Port-au-Prince (check contact information for the complete address). Your representative must bring • A letter from IRCC stating that you must get a Haitian police certificate • A copy of your national identity card • Two copies of a recent identification photograph • A copy of the first page of your passport (page with name and passport number) • A copy of your birth certificate ( Acte de naissance or Extrait des Archives Nationales ) • A copy of your marriage certificate if you’re married ( Acte de marriage ) • A copy of your fingerprints Fingerprints must be taken at a police station in your place of residence (subject to local rules and procedures) or at your nearest Haitian consulate. We don’t accept police certificates issued by regional police…

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Haiti
heading: Contact Information
text: Direction Centrale de la Police Judiciaire (DCPJ) Boulevard Toussaint Louverture Route de l'aéroport prolongée Clercine 6 Port-au-Prince Haïti

text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada and for some temporary residence programs (please see the Document Checklist and/or visa office requirements for details). • Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) will send you a letter requesting a police certificate after you send us your application if a Hong Kong police certificate is required. • You must include or submit that letter when you apply for a police certificate from the Hong Kong Police Force. • The Hong Kong Police Force will send your police certificate directly to the IRCC office at the Consulate General of Canada. You will not be required to send it separately.

Such certificates will not be accepted. • There are different formats for the police clearance certificate depending on the issuing authority.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Iceland
heading: How to get a police certificate – Iceland
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Iceland
heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for: • some temporary residence programs Check your application package or instructions for details.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Iceland
heading: How to apply
text: Apply online (available in English and Icelandic only) or in person at the district commissioner (Sýslumenn) in the municipality where you live in Iceland (check Contact information for details). Certificates are available to everyone over the age of 15 who has an Icelandic identification number (also called a kennitala). You can find more information about criminal records on the Government of Iceland website (available in English and Icelandic only). You must give written authority (signed by two witnesses) to a representative in Iceland to request the certificate on your behalf. The certificate request can be sent by fax or email as a PDF attachment to the district commissioner (Sýslumenn) in the municipality where you lived (check Contact information for details).

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Iceland
heading: Special considerations
text: If you are a citizen of Iceland, you do not need to provide a police certificate from this country with your application. However, an IRCC officer may request one at a later date. You must provide police certificates for all other countries or territories where you have lived for a total of six months or more since your 18 th birthday. If you don’t have an Icelandic identification number (kennitala), contact your district commissioner (available in English and Icelandic only) to find out how to get one.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Iceland
heading: Contact information
text: District commissioners’ office locations are available on the Government of Iceland website (available in English and Icelandic only).

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Hungary
heading: How to get a police certificate – Hungary
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada. You will also be requested to provide a police certificate to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) if you declared a criminal record on your application for an electronic travel authorization, work permit or study permit.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Hungary
heading: How to apply
text: There are three ways you can apply: • You can apply in person directly at the Customer Service Centre of the Criminal Records Authority at: Közigazgatási és Elektronikus Közszolgáltatások Központi Hivatala, KEKKH Ügyfélszolgálat, 1133 Budapest, Visegrádi utca 110-112 • You can apply by mail by sending your application form and payment to the Central Office for Administrative and Electronic Public Services at: Közigazgatási és Elektronikus Közszolgáltatások Központi Hivatala 1475 Budapest, Pf. 172. • You can apply online if you are already registered with the Customer Service Centre and possess a recognized electronic signature ( tanúsított elektronikus aláírás ) by applying through the website of the Central Office for Administrative and Electronic Public Services . You can apply at your nearest Hungarian embassy or consulate

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Hungary
heading: Special considerations
text: • The application form to request a certificate is available at all post offices. If you are sending by mail, you must include a postal order with your application. • Applications must be made by the person requesting the service. Representatives are currently not authorized. • You must select the following searches when completing the application form: Under section III, select “No” (Nem) • Under section V, check all of the following boxes: “B” ( büntetlen előéletű - no criminal record) • “C” ( nem áll közügyektől eltiltás hatálya alatt - no legal injunction in force against the applicant barring from participating in public affairs) • “D” ( nem áll foglalkozástól eltiltás hatálya alatt - no legal injunction in force against the applicant barring from accepting any professional occupation) • You must have your certificate translated into English or French.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Hungary
heading: Contact information
text: For more information on fees, processing times and on how to apply online, please consult the website of the Central Office for Administrative and Electronic Public Services .

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Iraq

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Israel
heading: How to get a police certificate – Israel
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Israel
heading: Name of the document you need
text: Teudat Meida Plili – Criminal Information Certificate If you live in Jerusalem (East or West), or in the Golan Heights, you should apply for an Israeli police certificate.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Israel
heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: You must provide a police certificate when you apply for: • some temporary residence programs Check your application package or instructions for details. Along with your Teudat Meida Plili, you must also provide • a self-declaration of past offences that are not on your Teudat Meida Plili • sentences that are not on your Teudat Meida Plili • the relevant dates of the offences, sentences, investigations and pardons that are not on your Teudat Meida Plili • all court documents for any criminal charges Provide complete copies of the decision or the resolution of the case. • proof you completed any penalties, such as paying a fine or serving prison time, for any previous criminal convictions

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Israel
heading: How to apply
text: You must have an open application with IRCC in order to apply in person for a police certificate at your nearest police station or to apply online via the Israeli Police website (available only in Hebrew). If you apply through a designated representative, they must submit a power of attorney for this purpose; however, the document does not need to be notarized. The police certificate will be sent directly to the Embassy of Canada in Tel Aviv to be entered into the IRCC system. This process takes approximately one month. You must contact IRCC Tel Aviv via email at taviv-immigration@international.gc.ca to verify that your application has been received and that all relevant information, such as your application number, UCI, full name, passport/travel document number, to support your application has been provided. The criminal information certificate can only be sent to the official entity requesting it. In accordance with Israeli law, IRCC Tel Aviv does not send, scan, transfer, provide Israeli PCs to requesters nor to third parties. As such, if your request is not linked to IRCC (e.g. Canadian employer in Canada), you will have to send an online request form from the Israeli Police to one of Israel’s representations in Canada and contact them for further assistance. You must have an open application with IRCC to apply for a police certificate online on the Israeli Police website (available only in Hebrew). The police certificate will be sent directly to the Embassy of Canada in Tel Aviv to be entered into the IRCC system. This process takes approximately one month. You must contact IRCC Tel Aviv via email at taviv-immigration@international.gc.ca to verify that your application has been received and that all relevant information, such as your application number, UCI, full…

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Israel

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Côte d’Ivoire
heading: How to apply
text: Citizens: Apply for a certificate online at https://servicepublic.gouv.ci/accueil/detaildemarcheparticulier/1/81/61 . You must provide your original birth certificate. You must also pay the applicable fees of 2500 cfa. Non-Citizens: If you are not a citizen of Côte d’Ivoire, apply for a certificate at the Department of Foreign Affairs. You will be required to present your residence certificate, your birth certificate and pay the applicable fees (1000 cfa). You must contact the closest Embassy or Consulate of the Republic of Côte d’Ivoire. You will need to provide a document stating the start date and end date of your residency along with the reason of your stay and your address.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Jordan
heading: How to get a police certificate - Jordan
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application. Do I need to provide a police certificate when applying for permanent residence? Yes. Name of document(s) to get : Jordan has two different types of police certificates, the Good Conduct certificate and the Non-conviction certificate . Both certificates are required.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Jordan
heading: How to get a Police Certificate:
text: The Good Conduct certificate can be obtained at the General Intelligence Department. There are offices located in cities across Jordan. The Non-conviction certificate can be obtained at the Courts of First Instance. There are offices located in cities across Jordan. You can apply for the Good Conduct certificate and the Non-conviction certificate through any Jordanian embassy abroad. See Information and documents you need for a list of required documents. The Good Conduct certificate can be obtained at the General Intelligence Department. There are offices located in cities across Jordan. The Non-conviction certificate can be obtained at the Courts of First Instance. There are offices located in cities across Jordan. You can apply for the Good Conduct certificate and the Non-conviction certificate through any Jordanian embassy abroad. See Information and documents you need for a list of required documents.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Jordan
heading: Special notes and considerations
text: Telephone: + 962 6 586 5131 Visit the GID website for more information on the Good Conduct certificate. Telephone: + 962 6 562 3320 Visit the Judicial Council website for more information on the Non-conviction certificate (available in Arabic only).

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Japan
heading: How to get a police certificate – Japan
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Japan
heading: Police certificates and IRCC requirements
text: You don’t need to provide a Police Certificate with your application. We will send you a letter requesting a Certificate of Criminal Record, if required, after you send your application for permanent residence, citizenship, or for some temporary resident programs.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Japan
heading: How to apply
text: After you receive the official request letter for a police certificate from IRCC, you must apply in person at the Prefectural Police Headquarters Identification Section where you live. When you go to the police headquarters, your fingerprints will be taken and you will be given an application form to complete. After you receive the official request letter for a police certificate from IRCC, you can apply at your local Japanese embassy or consulate. Please refer to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan website for a list of Japanese embassies, consulates and permanent missions. Your fingerprints will be taken and the application form for the Certificate of Criminal Record will be provided to you at the time and place where you apply.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Japan
heading: Special considerations
text: If you are a citizen of Japan, you do not need to provide a police certificate from this country with your application. However, an IRCC officer may request one at a later date. You must provide police certificates for all other countries or territories where you have lived for a total of 6 months or more since your 18th birthday. You (or a representative acting on your behalf, whom you must identify at the time you apply for the certificate) will receive the Certificate of Criminal Record in person. You do not need to have your certificate translated. If you are applying online, open the sealed envelope, scan the document and upload it to your file (please keep the original copy in case IRCC requests to see it). If you applied using a paper application, send the original to the office where your application is being processed.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Japan
heading: Contact information
text: Please refer to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan website for a list of Japanese embassies, consulates and permanent missions.

You can apply at your nearest embassy or consulate of Kazakhstan The certificate must include all names you have used while in Kazakhstan. heading: Police certificate and IRCC requirements
text: IRCC will send you a letter requesting a police certificate after you send your Application for Permanent Residence. You must bring that letter when you apply for a Police Certificate.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Kuwait
heading: How to apply
text: If you live in Kuwait: You must apply in person OR ask a representative (a friend, relative or colleague) in Kuwait, with a power of attorney, to apply on your behalf and bring: • The letter from the Embassy of Canada requesting the Good Conduct Certificate • One photograph (4 × 6 cm) • A copy of your passport and ID card If you live outside of Kuwait: • Return to Kuwait and apply in person OR ask a representative (a friend, relative or colleague) in Kuwait, with a power of attorney, to apply on your behalf • Apply at your nearest Kuwaiti Embassy or consulate You or your representative must bring: • The letter from the Embassy of Canada requesting the Good Conduct Certificate • A copy of your passport and ID card • Two photographs (4 × 6 cm)

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate – Kuwait
heading: Contact information
text: Ministry of the Interior General Department for Criminal Evidence Department of Identification and Automated Search Farwaniya-Dhajeej area Airport Road 55, opposite the Kuwait Airways main building. Phone: +965 2 434-6101. Fax: +965 2 434-5790.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Lebanon
heading: How to get a Police Certificate:
text: You should apply in person at your nearest gendarmerie territorial brigade, (except in those located in the Mouhafazat (Departments) centers and the Furn El Chebback brigade, see Special notes and considerations for complete list) and provide: • Identity card or civil status extract or a passport. A copy of the identity card or the civil status extract may also be presented, on condition that the commander of the brigade has personally noted the term “Certified copy” You should assign a relative (spouse, sibling, parent, or child over 18 years of age) who is living in Lebanon power of attorney and ask them to submit a request letter on your behalf to the commander of the judicial police. They must apply in person and bring: • Copy of your passport • Copy of your residency permit for Lebanon (if available) • A letter from you stating: Your residential address when you lived in Lebanon • Information about your employment/employer while in Lebanon (or the school you attended) • How long you lived in Lebanon (give exact dates: from (blank) to (blank)) The commander will forward your request to the criminal record department where your relative who submitted the letter can obtain the criminal record. For more information, visit the ISF Interior Security Forces website. You should apply in person at your nearest gendarmerie territorial brigade, (except in those located in the Mouhafazat (Departments) centers and the Furn El Chebback brigade) (see Special notes and considerations for complete list) and provide the following, depending on your nationality: • An entrance ticket (white) • Your Syrian identity document Arab or foreign nationals: • Your residence permit You should assign a relative (spouse, sibling, parent, or child over 18 years of age) who is living in Lebanon…

pageTitle: Police certificate
heading: Police certificate
text: • Who needs a police certificate • When to get a police certificate • How to get a police certificate Different programs have different time requirements for the submission of police certificates. If you are an Express Entry candidate, you have 60 days to submit your police certificates. Avoid delays by starting your police check as soon as you’re in the pool. Get more details about police certificates for Express Entry . If you’re an International Experience Canada candidate, in most cases you’ll need to provide a police certificate when you submit your work permit application. Get more details about police certificates for International Experience Canada . If you’re applying for citizenship, you need to submit a police certificate for the following citizenship applications: • resuming your Canadian citizenship (after giving it up) If you’re applying to any other immigration program, check the instruction guide for the program you are applying for.

pageTitle: Police certificate
heading: If you need a request letter
text: Some countries will give you a police certificate only if you have a request letter from us. If this applies to you upload a document in the police certificate field in the document checklist that says “I am applying from a country that requires an official request letter from IRCC to get a police certificate.” We’ll then review your application. If it is otherwise complete , we’ll send you more information on how to get a police certificate.

pageTitle: Police certificate
heading: How long your police certificate is valid
text: The police certificate for the country where you currently reside must be issued no more than 6 months before the date you submit your application. As processing times may vary, we may ask you for updated certificates. For any other country, the police certificate must be issued after the last time you resided there for 6 consecutive months or longer since the age of 18. Some countries put expiry dates on their police certificates. If this is your situation, include the certificate. We’ll accept it if: • it was issued after the last time you stayed there for 6 consecutive months or longer • it is not for the country where you currently reside IRCC may require updated documentation at any time during processing. You don’t need to give us police certificates for any period of time before you were 18 years old or for time spent in Canada.

pageTitle: Police certificate
heading: Submit scanned copies of the original certificates
text: Police certificates must be scanned copies of the original certificate(s) in colour. Certified true copies or unauthorized copies won’t be accepted and will result in your application being rejected.

pageTitle: Police certificate
heading: Police certificate
text: • Who needs a police certificate • When to get a police certificate • How to get a police certificate You and any family member 18 or older may need to get a police certificate, depending on the program you’re applying to. You must provide a police certificate if you apply for: • International Experience Canada If you have a prior criminal record, you may need to provide a police certificate for any other programs. Check the program’s document checklist or local visa office requirements for details.

pageTitle: How to Get a Police Certificate - Burma (Myanmar)
heading: How to Get a Police Certificate - Burma (Myanmar)

pageTitle: How to Get a Police Certificate - Burma (Myanmar)
heading: How to apply
text: Apply in person at your local township police station. You can’t get a certificate unless you’re a resident. If you don’t live in Burma (Myanmar) anymore, you don’t provide a police certificate from there. If you’re asked for a police certificate from Burma (Myanmar), write: “I cannot provide a police certificate for the time I spent in Burma (Myanmar) because certificates are only available to people who currently live in Burma (Myanmar).”

pageTitle: How to Get a Police Certificate – Northern Ireland
heading: How to Get a Police Certificate – Northern Ireland
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

pageTitle: How to get a police certificate - Mauritius
heading: How to get a police certificate - Mauritius
text: If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application. Do I need to provide a police certificate when applying for permanent residence? Yes. How and where do I obtain one? If you are a citizen of Mauritius , you must provide the original and a photocopy of: • your birth certificate • your marriage certificate (if you are a woman) If you are a foreign resident , you must provide the original and a photocopy of: • your proof of residency • any other additional document (e.g., an identity card) if available No fee is required and no application form needs to be filled out. Only the documents mentioned above are required. The Director of Public Prosecutions 2nd Floor Renganaden Seeneevassen Building Jules Koenig Street Port Louis, Mauritius

pageTitle: Withdraw (cancel) your application
heading: Withdraw (cancel) your application
text: Your IRCC application Asking to withdraw or cancel your application means you’re asking us to permanently stop processing it. We’ll review your request and determine if you can withdraw your application.

pageTitle: Withdraw (cancel) your application
heading: When to ask to withdraw your application
text: You can ask to withdraw your application if: • we haven’t started processing it, or • we are still processing it Processing starts when we determine your application is complete and ends when we make a final decision on it. If you ask to withdraw your application after we finish processing it, we may refuse your request. We may also refuse your withdrawal request if your application is undergoing additional review.

pageTitle: Withdraw (cancel) your application
heading: You may not get a full refund if you withdraw
text: Whether you get a full refund depends on when you ask to withdraw and what you applied for. Generally, once we start processing your application, we will only refund some fees. Some applications, like electronic travel authorizations (eTAs), don’t have any fees we can refund. If you applied and paid online, you don’t have to ask for a refund. We’ll issue a refund to you for the fees that can be refunded. If you did not apply and pay online, you may need to ask for a fee refund. We will refund only some fees, such as: • the right of permanent residence fee (RPRF) • the right of citizenship fee • the open work permit fee (for labour market impact assessment–exempt [LMIA-exempt] workers only) • the employer compliance fee (for employers only) • International Experience Canada (IEC) fees ( read more about refunds for IEC ) • If we approve your application, we won’t refund your fees. • If we refuse your application, we will refund only some fees. How to request a refund

pageTitle: Withdraw (cancel) your application
heading: How you withdraw depends on the application you submitted
text: You need to send us a written request to withdraw your application. We’ll review your request and determine if you can withdraw your application. You can withdraw most applications using our web form, but some have specific instructions you must follow. Check if your application has specific instructions before you send us a request. There are specific instructions you need to follow to withdraw these applications. Citizenship-related applications Family members of victims of flight PS752 Family sponsorship: Spouse, partner or child Family sponsorship: Parent or grandparent Family sponsorship: Other relatives Foreign nationals, with family in Canada, who are affected by the conflict in Sudan Permanent residence for foreign nationals who were in state care Permanent residence for Ukrainian nationals with family in Canada Permanent resident card and permanent resident travel document Blended Visa Office-Referred Program Pre-removal risk assessments Sponsorship agreement holders International Experience Canada If there aren’t specific instructions for your application, send us a request to withdraw using our web form. Keep a copy of your request for your records. Under Tell us about your request : • include a clear statement that you want to withdraw your application • explain why you want to withdraw your application • include the date you submitted your application • include your payment receipt number, if you know it • We’ll review your request and determine if you can withdraw your application. • We’ll contact you if we need more information or refuse your withdrawal request if your application is undergoing additional review. • If you can withdraw your application, we’ll issue a refund for the fees that can be refunded ( some fees can be refunded and some can’t ).…

pageTitle: Change to citizenship rules in 2025
heading: Change to citizenship rules in 2025
text: On December 15, 2025, the Government of Canada passed Bill C-3, An Act to amend the Citizenship Act . This legislation changes the first-generation limit to citizenship by descent. On this page, we explain what this change means, how to check if you’re affected, and what you need to do before travelling to Canada. This change officially took effect on December 15, 2025 .

pageTitle: Change to citizenship rules in 2025
heading: What the change means
text: Before Bill C-3, Canadian citizenship by descent was limited to the first generation born outside Canada. This rule was called the first-generation limit to citizenship. The changes made by Bill C-3 removed this limit in some situations. This change to the first-generation limit doesn’t affect anyone who already became a Canadian citizen before Bill C-3 became law. If you were already a citizen before the bill, you’re still a citizen. To count generations for this rule, we consider the first generation to be the first person born or adopted outside Canada to a Canadian citizen. • Canadian citizen: Born in Canada (a citizen by birth) or applied for and was granted citizenship (a citizen through naturalization). • First generation: Born outside Canada to a Canadian citizen who was born in Canada or granted citizenship (naturalized). This person is Canadian by descent. • Second generation or later: Also born outside Canada to a first-generation person. This person is now Canadian in some situations. This is also how we count generations for people who are adopted and apply for a direct grant of citizenship. People born outside Canada in the second generation (or later) may be Canadian if • their parent was also born or adopted outside Canada to a Canadian citizen (meaning the grandparent was Canadian), and • that same parent spent at least 1,095 days in Canada before the birth Adopted people are likely eligible to apply for Canadian citizenship through a direct grant for adopted people if • they were born and adopted outside Canada in the second generation or later, and • their Canadian parent spent at least 1,095 days in Canada before the adoption Citizenship may have been restored or given to people who were born outside Canada in the second generation or later before…

pageTitle: Change to citizenship rules in 2025
heading: If you applied under the interim measure
text: In December 2023, the Ontario Superior Court of Justice declared the first-generation limit, as it was at that time, unconstitutional for many people. In response, we announced an interim measure to support those affected. Now that the Citizenship Act has been amended, we will process your application using the new rules. You don’t need to submit a new citizenship certificate application.

pageTitle: Change to citizenship rules in 2025
heading: How to prove your Canadian citizenship
text: If you think you became a citizen because of Bill C-3, you must apply for a citizenship certificate to find out for sure. You may also want a certificate as proof of your citizenship if you became a citizen automatically. When you apply, we’ll review your application to check if you’re a Canadian citizen. If you are, we’ll give you a citizenship certificate (proof of your Canadian citizenship). This proof will help you apply for a Canadian passport and avoid delays at the border if you plan to enter Canada . Apply for a citizenship certificate. If you’re not affected by this change, but you still want to become a Canadian citizen, you can still apply for citizenship if you’re eligible. Find out if you’re eligible to apply for citizenship .

pageTitle: Change to citizenship rules in 2025
heading: Before travelling to Canada
text: If you think you may be a Canadian citizen, apply for proof of your citizenship, then get a Canadian passport to avoid delays at the border. • First, apply for a citizenship certificate to prove your Canadian citizenship. • If you’re a citizen, use your certificate to apply for a Canadian passport . • Then you can enter Canada using your Canadian passport. Make sure that you prepare the documents you need to travel to Canada • learn how to enter Canada as a dual citizen

pageTitle: Citizenship ceremony
heading: Citizenship ceremony
text: For most citizenship applicants, the citizenship ceremony is the last step before becoming a Canadian citizen. If you’re a citizenship candidate, the ceremony is • a special, dignified and meaningful occasion where you take the oath of citizenship • an opportunity to reflect on the rights and responsibilities of citizenship • celebrate with your family and friends

pageTitle: Citizenship ceremony
heading: Features
text: As a member of the public or an elected official, you can attend most in-person ceremonies to support new citizens.

pageTitle: Check if you may be a citizen
heading: Check if you may be a citizen
text: On December 15, 2025, the Government of Canada passed Bill C-3 to change the first-generation limit on citizenship by descent. The Ontario Superior Court of Justice ruled in December 2023 that the limit was unconstitutional for many people. In March 2025, we introduced an interim measure to support those affected. We will now process all applications submitted under the interim measure using the new rules. Learn more about this change. Depending on your situation, you might already be a Canadian citizen based on the citizenship rules. This page can help you learn more about this possibility, including how to find out for sure. If you plan to travel soon, get proof of your citizenship and a Canadian passport before you travel to avoid delays at the border.

pageTitle: Check if you may be a citizen
heading: If you were born in Canada
text: You’re likely a Canadian citizen if you were born in Canada. When you’re born here, your province or territory issues a birth certificate. These documents are valid proof of your Canadian citizenship. Some people born in Canada are not citizens. For example, if your parent was working in Canada with diplomatic privileges for a foreign government or international organization when you were born, then you weren’t a Canadian citizen at birth.

pageTitle: Check if you may be a citizen
heading: If you were born outside Canada
text: Some people born outside Canada may be Canadian citizens. If you were born outside of Canada before December 15, 2025 , you’re likely a Canadian citizen if your parent was also a Canadian citizen when you were born. If you were born outside of Canada on or after December 15, 2025 , to a Canadian parent also born outside of Canada, you’re likely a Canadian citizen if • your parent was also a Canadian citizen when you were born, and • your Canadian parent spent at least 1,095 days in Canada before you were born You’re not automatically a Canadian citizen if your parent was granted Canadian citizenship after you were born. You’re not automatically a Canadian citizen if you were born outside Canada and adopted by a Canadian citizen. After the adoption is complete, you or your parent can submit an application for citizenship. Learn more about international adoption. You’re a Canadian citizen if • we granted you citizenship as an adult • we granted you citizenship as a minor because a parent or legal guardian applied for you When we grant you Canadian citizenship, we issue a citizenship certificate that can be used as proof of your citizenship.

pageTitle: Check if you may be a citizen
heading: How changes to the Citizenship Act may affect you
text: It’s possible that changes to the Citizenship Act gave or restored citizenship to you. Based on these changes, you may be a Canadian citizen if • you were born outside Canada and your parent was a citizen when you were born • you were born outside Canada and your parent became a citizen because of these changes (even after your birth) • you previously lost your citizenship Learn about changes to the Citizenship Act.

pageTitle: Check if you may be a citizen
heading: How to confirm if you’re a Canadian citizen
text: You can apply for a Canadian citizenship certificate if you • don’t have proof of your Canadian citizenship • lost your documents or they were stolen • are not sure if you’re a Canadian citizen and want us to check This proof will help you apply for a Canadian passport and avoid delays at the border if you plan to enter Canada . We’ll review your application to check if you’re a Canadian citizen. If you are, we’ll issue you a citizenship certificate (proof of your Canadian citizenship). This certificate is valid proof that you’re a citizen. Apply for a citizenship certificate . If you’re not a Canadian citizen, you may still be eligible to apply for a grant of Canadian citizenship. Apply for citizenship.

pageTitle: Check if you may be a citizen
heading: Before travelling to Canada
text: If you think you may be a Canadian citizen, apply for proof of your citizenship, then get a Canadian passport to avoid delays at the border. • First, apply for a citizenship certificate to prove your Canadian citizenship. • If you’re a citizen, use your certificate to apply for a Canadian passport . • Then you can enter Canada using your Canadian passport. Make sure that you prepare the documents you need to travel to Canada • learn how to enter Canada as a dual citizen

pageTitle: Record your trips outside Canada
heading: Record your trips outside Canada
text: As a temporary or permanent resident, it’s a good idea to write down your trips outside Canada by air, land or boat, including day trips to the United States. You’ll need this information when you apply for a new Permanent Residence card and when you apply for citizenship . The travel journal is an easy way to record trips outside Canada. Keep it with your passport or other travel documents. This is not an official document and we don’t need to see it. It’s just to help you remember your trips. You won’t need to send it to us with your application. Download the travel journal

pageTitle: Situations that may prevent you from becoming a Canadian citizen
heading: Situations that may prevent you from becoming a Canadian citizen
text: You cannot become a citizen if you’re prohibited under the Citizenship Act . For example, if • you’re in Canada serving a term of imprisonment • you’re serving a sentence outside Canada • you’re charged with, on trial for, or involved in an appeal for an offence under the Citizenship Act , or an indictable offence in Canada • committed outside Canada that’s equivalent to an indictable offence in Canada • you’re under a removal order (Canadian officials asked you to leave Canada) • you’re being investigated for, are charged with, on trial for, involved in an appeal for or have been convicted of a war crime, or • a crime against humanity • you had a citizenship application refused for misrepresentation in the past 5 years • you had your Canadian citizenship revoked (taken away) because of fraud in the past 10 years • you’ve been convicted of an indictable offence in Canada or an offence under the Citizenship Act , and if we received your application after June 11, 2015, and this conviction took place in the 4 years before you apply • in the 4 years before you apply, you were convicted of an offence outside Canada that’s equivalent to an indictable offence in Canada. This applies even if you were pardoned or granted amnesty • regardless of when we receive your application • while a permanent resident, you were convicted of terrorism, high treason, treason or spying offences • served as a member of an armed force of a country or territory, or an organized armed group, that’s engaged in armed conflict with Canada If you’re not sure whether the above situations apply to you, contact your lawyer or arresting police officer. If any of the above situations apply to you • wait until the situation no longer applies before you apply for citizenship Exception: You can never resume…

pageTitle: People registered under the Indian Act
heading: People registered under the Indian Act
text: Apply for Canadian citizenship If you’re registered under the Indian Act , you may already be a Canadian citizen . Even if you’re not, you have the right to be in Canada. We’re working on a new section that will help adults (18 and above) and minors (under 18) registered under the Indian Act understand how they can become Canadian citizens. Once ready, it will explain • who is eligible for this application process and what requirements they must meet • the required forms and documents • how to pay the fees and submit an application • what to do after you submit an application While we work on the new section, you can review our processing guidance on citizenship for people registered under the Indian Act .

pageTitle: People registered under the Indian Act
heading: Other citizenship applications
text: Even if you don’t meet the requirements to use this process, other options may be available to you. Check the eligibility requirements for our other application processes: • Canadian armed forces

pageTitle: Apply for citizenship: Canadian Armed Forces
heading: Apply for citizenship: Canadian Armed Forces
text: Find out how COVID-19 affects your citizenship application If you’re a current or past member of the Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) you can apply for citizenship using a fast-track process . This fast-track option lets us speed up the citizenship application process for: • permanent residents serving in the CAF • foreign military members on exchange with the CAF You can use this application process if you are or were a: • foreign military member who is or was attached or seconded to the CAF

pageTitle: Apply for citizenship: Canadian Armed Forces
heading: Eligibility
text: To be eligible, you must: • meet all of the standard conditions to apply for citizenship (except for the residency requirement) • have completed three years (1,095 days) of service in or with the CAF in the six years (2,190 days) right before the date of your application • have met your personal income tax filing obligations in three taxation years that are fully or partially within the six years right before the date you apply If you are no longer a member of the CAF, you must have been released honourably. If you were released other than honourably, you aren’t eligible to apply using the fast-track process. If you were a foreign military member attached or seconded to the CAF, you don’t need to: • be a permanent resident • meet the tax filing obligation If you’ve served for one year less than the standard residence requirement for citizenship , you may still qualify. The length of time you served in the CAF is used instead of the length of time you lived in Canada. You’re able to count your time as a Regular or Reserve Force member regardless of if you were being paid as a full-time member or not. You can count time without pay as long as you were still a CAF member.

heading: Features
text: The physical presence calculator will be unavailable from 10:00 am to 10:15 am Eastern time, on Thursday, August 6, 2020 in order to perform system maintenance. Our online services are unavailable. Please try again later. Thank you for your patience. • Celebrate being Canadian

pageTitle: Give up (renounce) Canadian citizenship: About the process
heading: Processing times
text: Processing times will vary based on: • the type of application submitted • if the application is complete • how quickly we expect to process applications we’ve already received • how easily we can verify your information • how long you take to respond to any requests or concerns Learn how processing times are calculated . If you renounce your Canadian citizenship, you lose all the rights and privileges of being a Canadian citizen and will have no status in Canada. This means that if you want to return to Canada permanently, you will have to apply for a permanent resident visa. If you want to return to Canada temporarily (to visit, work or study), you will have to apply for a temporary resident visa, if applicable.

pageTitle: Be active in your community
heading: Be active in your community
text: • Be active in your community • Participate in a reaffirmation ceremony • Host a citizenship ceremony Participating actively in society is a part of your rights and responsibilities as a Canadian citizen or newcomer to Canada. When you participate in your community, you help build a stronger and more inclusive Canada. Here is how you can be an active citizen: • participate in local cultural and community events • vote and participate in Canada’s democracy • volunteer in schools or local charities • protect the environment • contribute to reconciliation with Indigenous peoples • engage in respectful and meaningful conversations with your peers about current events • welcome new Canadians in your community Share your ideas with us and show us how you’re an active citizen on Facebook and Twitter . Use the hashtag #MyCitizenship . Need inspiration? Find out how immigrants make a difference for Canada .

pageTitle: Citizenship test
heading: Citizenship test
text: Most people need to take a test as part of the citizenship application. Find out how to prepare for the test and what to expect after you take it.

pageTitle: Apply for Canadian citizenship: Stateless people
heading: Apply for Canadian citizenship: Stateless people
text: This citizenship application is only for stateless people born to a Canadian parent. A person is stateless if no country or state recognizes them as a citizen or national under its laws.

pageTitle: Adoption authorities contacts
heading: Government of Canada (federal)
text: The federal government is only responsible for the immigration or citizenship process of an adopted child. Under the Hague Convention , Canada’s federal central authority is Intercountry Adoption Services, which is part of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada. The role of Intercountry Adoption Services is to • collect and distribute adoption information to Canadian provincial and territorial central authorities • help with communication, cooperation and coordination between federal and provincial and territorial central authorities • within the federal government • with foreign central authorities or adoption authorities • help to address issues such as unethical and irregular adoption practices Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Intercountry Adoption Services Family and Social Immigration IA-Inquiries@cic.gc.ca
