pageTitle: Closed: Temporary public policies: Temporary resident to permanent resident pathway – How to apply
heading: How to apply
text: Immigration Representatives As with other immigration programs, representatives may continue to provide guidance and assist their clients as they navigate all aspects of the requirements of this pathway. The only part of the process that representatives can’t do is open a portal account or electronically sign the application for their clients. The dedicated portal is a tool to be used by clients to upload IRCC forms and supporting documents required for the immigration decision. Representatives could certainly help prepare the documents applicants must upload to the portal. In other words, an immigration consultant or lawyer may assist clients with their application, but they should not log in to the portal using a client’s credentials or sign the application for them. In all situations where authorized representatives assist clients virtually (through sharing screens) for this initiative, the client, after reading the attestation, needs to be the one who checks the box confirming they have read and understood the attestation, types their name and be the one clicking the “submit” button. These actions combined – by the client - constitute for us the legal requirement for applications to be considered “signed”, as per the legislative and regulatory requirements. • Make sure you’re eligible • Create your account and document checklist • Get your fingerprints and photo taken • Submit your application Be sure to review the detailed eligibility requirements . If you don’t meet the requirements of at least one stream or are missing any required documents, your application for permanent residence will be refused. You will not receive a refund of your application fees. Read the Instruction Guide for important information about how to complete the application, fees and how to…

pageTitle: Closed: Permanent residence for Ukrainian nationals with family members in Canada
heading: Closed: Permanent residence for Ukrainian nationals with family members in Canada
text: We’re not accepting new applications. This temporary public policy closed on October 22, 2024. We’ll continue processing the applications we received on or before this date. If you’re eligible, you can still apply for • temporary residence immigration measures to study, work, or extend your status in Canada • permanent residence through our regular programs We put a temporary public policy in place to help families affected by the Russian invasion of Ukraine reunite in Canada. This policy let certain Ukrainian foreign nationals in Canada and their family members apply for permanent residence. • Family members can live in or outside Ukraine. • Permanent resident visas will be issued to family members living outside of Canada. Deadline: October 22, 2024

pageTitle: Immigration measures for Ukrainians and family members: Extend your open work permit in Canada
heading: Immigration measures for Ukrainians and family members: Extend your open work permit in Canada
text: As of April 1, 2026: Special measures for Ukrainians and their eligible family members have changed. If you’re a Ukrainian national or an eligible family member of a Ukrainian and you arrived in Canada on or before December 31, 2024, you may be eligible to extend your open work permit for up to 3 years. If you want to extend your stay in Canada, you need to apply for another temporary resident permit (TRP) through the regular application process, even if the issue that made you inadmissible has been fixed. If you have an open work permit that you want to extend, you can apply to extend it together with your TRP application. Deadline to apply: March 31, 2027

pageTitle: Immigration measures for Ukrainians and family members: Extend your open work permit in Canada
heading: How to extend your open work permit
text: To find out if you’re eligible and how to apply, tell us when you arrived in Canada: • on or before March 31, 2024 • between April 1, 2024, and December 31, 2024 You may extend your status if you meet all of the following requirements: • You arrived in Canada on or before March 31, 2024. • You have valid temporary resident status in Canada. Not including temporary resident permit holders. • You have a valid open work permit that was issued under a public policy for Ukrainians and eligible family members of Ukrainians . • You’re in Canada when you apply for your work permit extension, and • a decision is made on your work permit extension • This will be your first time being issued a work permit extension under these new special measures. You can only extend your work permit under these new measures once. Public policies are put in place to provide temporary programs or services to the public. To be eligible for these measures, you must have a valid work permit that was issued under one of the following public policies: • Temporary public policy to facilitate continued access to open work permits and study permits for foreign nationals in Canada who arrived under the Canada–Ukraine authorization for emergency travel measures – Extension 16 February 2026 • ARCHIVED – Temporary public policy to facilitate continued access to open work permits and study permits for foreign nationals in Canada who arrived under the Canada–Ukraine authorization for emergency travel measures 8 August 2024 • ARCHIVED – Temporary public policy for foreign nationals who applied under the Canada–Ukraine authorization for emergency travel measures and for new temporary resident applicants 7 July 2023 • ARCHIVED – Temporary public policy to renew the Canada–Ukraine Authorization of Emergency Travel…

pageTitle: Find out if your representative is authorized
heading: Find out if your representative is authorized
text: • Learn about representatives • How to choose an immigration or citizenship representative • Find out if your representative is authorized • Add, change or cancel a representative • Give someone access to your application • File a complaint against a representative March is Fraud Prevention Month – read the Minister’s statement on fraud prevention in Canada. You can check if a person is licensed to represent immigrants or to give advice. • Citizenship or immigration consultants must be a member of the College of Immigration and Citizenship Consultants . • Lawyers or notaries must be a member of a Canadian provincial or territorial law society , or the Chambre des notaires du Québec. • Paralegals (Ontario only) must be members of the Law Society of Ontario If they are not members in good standing, you should not use their services. Most law societies let you check online to see if a person is a member in good standing.

pageTitle: Closed: Immediate or extended family or de facto dependant of a resettled Yazidi or survivor of Daesh
heading: Closed: Immediate or extended family or de facto dependant of a resettled Yazidi or survivor of Daesh
text: We’ve reached the application limit for this program. We have accepted 400 applications and are no longer accepting new submissions. We’re still processing applications in our inventory and applicants will continue to arrive through 2026-2027. We’re helping Yazidis and survivors of Daesh who’ve resettled in Canada reunite with family who are still in Iraq.

pageTitle: Closed: Immediate or extended family or de facto dependant of a resettled Yazidi or survivor of Daesh
heading: How this initiative works
text: • Completed We ask eligible Yazidis and survivors of Daesh in Canada to fill out a web form if they had family in Iraq they wanted to bring to Canada. • Completed We close web form submissions once we receive enough submissions to fill the available spots. • Completed We email eligible individuals in Canada who submitted a web form to get more information about their family in Iraq • their consent for the International Organization for Migration (IOM) to contact the members of their family in Iraq If an individual in Canada doesn’t appear eligible, they have 30 days to provide us with proof to show that they are. • Completed The IOM in Iraq contacts eligible family in Iraq and sends us their application. • Completed If the number of web form submissions exceeds the number of available spots, applicants will be informed that their submission will not be processed once the program has closed. • In progress We process the applications and issue permanent resident visas to successful applicants. • In progress Successful applicants come to Canada as government-assisted refugees. They’ll get income and other support for up to 1 year • access to resettlement support from the Resettlement Assistance Program and services from the Settlement Program • pre-departure and in-Canada benefits from the Interim Federal Health Program

pageTitle: Closed: Immediate or extended family or de facto dependant of a resettled Yazidi or survivor of Daesh
heading: Who can apply
text: Both you and your family in Iraq (the applicant) need to meet certain requirements: • currently live in Canada • be a citizen or permanent resident of Canada • have come to Canada under the 2016–2017 initiative for survivors of Daesh or • 2017 or 2019 public policies as a government-assisted refugee Your family (the principal applicant) must be either • spouse or common-law partner • dependent child of the applicant or of the applicant’s spouse or partner • a dependent child of a dependent child • child of any age (of either you or your spouse or common-law partner) • great aunt or great uncle • child of an aunt or an uncle • current or former legal guardian • is considered a key member of your family unit, and is emotionally or economically dependent on you, or • is a combination of both that makes them a de facto member of your family, and doesn’t meet the above definition of a family member under the Immigration and Refugee Protection Regulations • be a national of Iraq • live in Iraq, both when they apply under the public policy • when we make a decision on their application • not plan to live in the province of Quebec • not be inadmissible to Canada Extended family members and de facto dependants must also provide a statutory declaration of their relationship to you.

pageTitle: Closed: Permanent residence for Ukrainian nationals with family members in Canada
heading: Closed: Permanent residence for Ukrainian nationals with family members in Canada
text: • Apply for an open work permit, study permit or to extend your stay in Canada If you submitted or were included in an application for the permanent residence pathway for Ukrainian nationals with family members in Canada, you may be able to apply for • to stay in Canada longer as a temporary resident This new public policy will let you work, study or stay in Canada longer as a temporary resident while your permanent residence application is being processed. What would you like to apply for? • Extend my stay as a visitor

pageTitle: Closed: Permanent residence for Ukrainian nationals with family members in Canada
heading: Open work permit
text: • for a new open work permit • to extend your open work permit, or • to restore your status and apply for a work permit Apply for a work permit Apply for a new study permit under this public policy. Apply for a study permit Apply to extend your study permit through our regular application process if you currently hold a valid study permit. Extend your study permit If you lost your status less than 90 days ago, you may be able to apply to restore it and get a study permit through our regular application process. You may be eligible to restore your status and apply for a new study permit under this public policy if at the time we received the permanent residence application you • had valid temporary resident status, or • were out of status for less than 90 days Deadline If your status expired more than 90 days ago, you must apply to restore it on or before March 31, 2026 . Apply for a study permit under the public policy Apply to extend your visitor status through our regular application process if you • currently have valid temporary resident status • are not applying for a work or study permit If you lost your temporary resident status less than 90 days ago, you may be eligible to apply to restore it through our regular application process. How to restore my status You may be eligible to restore your status under this public policy if at the time we received the permanent residence application, you • had valid temporary resident status, or • were out of status for less than 90 days Deadline If your status expired more than 90 days ago, you must apply to restore it on or before March 31, 2026 . Restore your status under the public policy

pageTitle: Crisis in Palestine
heading: Crisis in Palestine
text: Be careful of suspicious sources promising to help you leave Gaza! Scammers may use fake phone numbers that look like ours to ask you for money. These are not agents or consultants acting on our behalf. We’ll never ask you for payment by telephone. If someone claiming to be from IRCC requests payment this way, it's a scam! If you’re not sure if something is a scam, you can • learn more about fraud and how to protect yourself Temporary measures are in place for people affected by the conflict in Palestine. • Special measures for family in Gaza Closed to new applications • What happens after you apply • What to do when you arrive in Canada • Temporary health care coverage • Apply for an open work permit or a study permit, or to stay in Canada longer Updated • General questions and information • Update the contact information on your application • If you have a valid visa and need help leaving Gaza • If you’re in Egypt after leaving Gaza and need to finalize your visa application • I submitted a crisis web form for the Gaza special measures and haven’t received a response. What should I do? • How do I get a temporary resident document with the “MOME2023” keyword on it? • I need a certificate for the Interim Federal Heath Program (IFHP). How do I get one?

pageTitle: Crisis in Israel and Palestine: Temporary health care coverage
heading: Crisis in Israel and Palestine: Temporary health care coverage
text: Updated eligibility requirements You may be eligible for temporary, limited health care coverage through the Interim Federal Health Program (IFHP) if you meet one of the situations below. Select the option below that best describes you.

pageTitle: Crisis in Israel and Palestine: Temporary health care coverage
heading: Who can get IFHP coverage
text: You’re eligible for the IFHP if you meet all of the following requirements: • You left Gaza on or after September 1, 2023. • You arrived in Canada by December 31, 2026. Updated • You’re in Canada with valid temporary resident status. If you’re eligible, you automatically get coverage. After your temporary residence application is approved, we’ll send you an IFHP certificate. You must have this certificate to get health products and services covered under the IFHP. If you didn’t get an IFHP certificate when your application was approved, you can contact us to request one. To get products and services, you need to • sign your certificate • find a health care provider that offers services under the IFHP program • show your certificate to the health care provider before you get a service or product Learn more about how to get health products and services . Length of coverage: 3 months, starting on the date you enter Canada (regardless of the date on your certificate) Coverage: You get in-Canada IFHP coverage, including • basic, supplementary and prescription drug coverage • the cost of 1 immigration medical exam Learn more about IFHP coverage .

pageTitle: Crisis in Israel and Palestine: Temporary health care coverage
heading: Who can get IFHP coverage
text: You’re eligible for the IFHP if you meet all of the following requirements: • You left Gaza on or after September 1, 2023. • You arrived in Canada by December 31, 2026. Updated • You’re in Canada with valid temporary resident status. If you're eligible, you automatically get coverage. After your temporary residence application is approved, we’ll send you an IFHP certificate. You must have this certificate to get health products and services covered under the IFHP. If you have an existing TRV, super visa or TRP and you didn’t get an IFHP certificate, you need to contact us to request one . To get eligible products and services, you need to • sign your certificate • find a health care provider that offers services under the IFHP program • show your certificate to the health care provider before you get a service or product Learn more about how to get health products and services . Length of coverage: 3 months, starting on the date you enter Canada (regardless of the date on your certificate) Coverage: You get in-Canada IFHP coverage, including • basic, supplementary and prescription drug coverage • the cost of 1 immigration medical exam Learn more about IFHP coverage .

pageTitle: Crisis in Israel and Palestine: Temporary health care coverage
heading: Who can get IFHP coverage
text: You’re eligible for the IFHP if you meet all of the following requirements: • You’re a permanent resident of Canada who left Israel or Palestine on or after October 7, 2023. • You arrived in Canada as part of the assisted departures out of Israel or Palestine. • You’re not able to get provincial or territorial health coverage right away. • You’re not able to get another type of public or private health care coverage. You need to apply for the IFHP. If your application is approved, we send you an IFHP certificate. The certificate proves you’re eligible for coverage. • Read the instruction guide . • Download and fill out the form: Application for IFHP Coverage (IMM 5564) (opens in a new tab) . Print it or save it on your computer. • You can get help opening an application form or completing an application form in the Help Centre. Provide copies of the following documents to support your application: • the photo or identification page of your passport or travel document • documents showing the date you left Israel or Palestine, for example: a flight travel itinerary Submit your application online. Follow these steps to apply : • Create an IRCC secure account. • Under the heading “what would you like to do today,” select “apply for the Interim Federal Health Program (IFHP).” • Upload your saved application form. • You’ll receive a response to your application by email through your account. To get products and services, you need to • sign your certificate • find a health care provider that offers services under the IFHP program • show your certificate to the health care provider before you get a service or product Learn more about how to get health products and services . Length of coverage: 3 months, starting on the date you enter Canada (regardless of the date on your…

pageTitle: Crisis in Israel and Palestine: Temporary health care coverage
heading: Who can get IFHP coverage
text: You’re eligible for the IFHP if you meet the all of the following requirements: • You’re the family member of a Canadian citizen or permanent resident of Canada. • You arrived in Canada as part of the assisted departures out of Israel or Palestine. • You’re in Canada with valid temporary resident status. • You’re not able to get another type of public or private health care coverage. A family member is defined as a • spouse or common-law partner • dependent child (yours, your spouse’s or your common-law partner’s) or • dependent child of a dependent child You need to apply for the IFHP. If your application is approved, we send you an IFHP certificate. The certificate proves you’re eligible for coverage. • Read the instruction guide . • Download and fill out the form: Application for IFHP Coverage (IMM 5564) (opens in a new tab) . Print it or save it on your computer. • You can get help opening an application form or completing an application form in the Help Centre. Provide copies of the following documents to support your application: • the photo or identification page of your passport or travel document • documents showing the date you left Israel or Palestine, for example: a flight travel itinerary Submit your application online. Follow these steps to apply : • Create an IRCC secure account. • Under the heading “what would you like to do today,” select “apply for the Interim Federal Health Program (IFHP).” • Upload your saved application form. • You’ll receive a response to your application by email through your account. To get products and services, you need to: • sign your certificate • find a health care provider that offers services under the IFHP program • show your certificate to the health care provider before you get a service or product Learn more about how…

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Financial assistance
heading: Crisis in Gaza: Financial assistance
text: Updated eligibility requirements Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada is giving financial assistance to help eligible individuals and families who are impacted by the crisis in Gaza and are in Canada with valid temporary resident status. The financial assistance is a non-taxable, one-time payment to help take care of your immediate and basic needs while you get settled in Canada. To make sure those who are already in Canada can quickly access financial assistance to meet their basic needs, we’re providing the one-time payments in stages. We’re now accepting applications from eligible applicants who arrive in Canada by December 31, 2026. If you arrive in Canada on or before this date, you must apply before March 31, 2027 to receive financial assistance. We encourage you to apply as soon as possible after arriving. How much can you expect to receive • CAN$3,000 for each adult (18 or older) • CAN$1,500 for each child (17 and under)

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Financial assistance
heading: Who can apply
text: You can apply if you meet the requirements for 1 of the 3 groups below. • You hold a passport or another travel document issued by the State of Palestine, and you left Gaza on or after September 1, 2023 • arrived in Canada by December 31, 2026 Updated • are in Canada with valid temporary resident status • You’re the immediate family member of someone under group 1, and you arrived in Canada by December 31, 2026 Updated • are in Canada with valid temporary resident status • You were part of the Government of Canada assisted departures out of Gaza or the West Bank between November 1, 2023, and December 31, 2024, and you are in Canada with valid temporary resident status Deadline to apply : March 31, 2027 You’re not eligible for financial assistance under this program if you submit a claim for refugee protection (asylum) in Canada . This is because you’re no longer considered a temporary resident while you wait for a decision on your asylum claim.

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Financial assistance
heading: Before you apply
text: If you don’t have a Canadian bank account, you need one to receive payment. This is because payment is made by direct deposit. We don’t provide financial assistance in cash. To open a bank account, you need your • temporary resident status document [IMM 1442] (opens in a new tab) This is your visitor record • temporary resident permit • passport (or other travel document) Learn more about opening a bank account in Canada (opens in a new tab) , including your rights and responsibilities.

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Financial assistance
heading: How to apply
text: If you meet the requirements, we’ll contact you by email with instructions on how to apply. In your email, we’ll give you • information on the financial assistance • instructions on how to apply • an email address for you to contact us to start your application If you didn’t get an email with instructions and you think you may be eligible, contact us using our crisis web form .

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Financial assistance
heading: Contact us using our crisis web form by following these instructions:
text: • Select “I need help with something else and I am impacted by the crisis in Israel and the Palestinian Territories.” • In the “Tell us about your situation” section enter the keyword “ CGTAI ” • tell us that you didn’t get an email with instructions on how to apply for financial assistance and you think you may be eligible • explain how you meet the eligibility requirements • Upload a copy of the bio page of your passport (or other valid travel document) • Upload a copy of your existing temporary resident document We’ll verify your information. If we determine you may be eligible, we’ll email you with instructions on how to apply. Go to our crisis web form

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Financial assistance
heading: Who is an immediate family member
text: An immediate family member is defined as a • spouse or common-law partner • dependent child (yours, your spouse’s or your common-law partner’s) or • dependent child of a dependent child After you contact us to start your application, we’ll send you • instructions on what information and supporting documents to send us Follow the instructions in your email and send us • your completed and signed application form • the required information and supporting documents indicated in your email and on the form

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Financial assistance
heading: After you apply
text: After we receive your completed application, we’ll send you a link to a secure web form. Use the secure web form to send us the information for the bank account where you want us to deposit the funds. Don’t send us your bank information by email. To set up direct deposit, we’ll need the following information: Your bank can give you this information either with a • direct deposit form This will have all 3 of these numbers, usually labelled. • void cheque This also has all 3 of the numbers, but they are not usually labelled. For security reasons, the link to the secure web form is generated only for you and expires after 10 business days. If your link expires, we’ll contact you and send you a new one. Find out more about the secure web form and how to make sure the email is from us . • Payment will be made by direct deposit in the bank account that was provided in the secure web form. • When your payment is processed, we’ll email you a “Notification of Payment Letter” and a copy of your signed application. • After your payment is processed, your bank may take 3 to 5 business days to show the deposit in your account. Contact us using the email address that was provided to you.

pageTitle: Situation in Iran: Temporary immigration measures for workers in Canada
heading: Situation in Iran: Temporary immigration measures for workers in Canada
text: As of March 1, 2026, special measures for Iranian nationals in Canada have changed . Temporary measures have been extended until March 31, 2027, for Iranian work permit holders in Canada only. Iranian nationals working in Canada may be eligible to extend their work permit. No other temporary measures are in place for Iranian applicants.

pageTitle: Situation in Iran: Temporary immigration measures for workers in Canada
heading: How to extend your stay as a worker
text: Deadline: Submit your application on or before You may extend your status if all of the following apply: • You’re an Iranian national with a valid passport. • You’re in Canada with a valid work permit when • you apply for your work permit extension , and • a decision is made on your application • Your valid work permit was issued no later than February 28, 2025. • You haven’t already been issued a work permit under the March 1, 2026, special measures to extend work permits. • If you were already issued a work permit extension, you can’t get a second one. • You meet the general eligibility and admissibility requirements for a work permit. To be eligible to apply for a work permit, you must • obey the law and have no record of criminal activity (we may ask you to give us a police clearance certificate ) • not be a danger to Canada’s security • be in good health and get a medical exam (if required to) • not plan to work for an employer listed as “ineligible” on the list of employers who failed to comply with the conditions • not plan to work for an employer that offers striptease, erotic dance, escort services or erotic massages on a regular basis • prove that you have enough money to pay for living expenses for you and the family members who came with you to Canada • transportation to return home for you and the family members who came with you to Canada • prove to an officer that you will leave Canada at the end of your authorized stay If you meet all the requirements, you can apply for a work permit extension that could be valid for up to 2 years. We can’t issue your work permit past the expiry date listed on your passport. If your passport will expire within 2 years, you should renew it before you apply. Follow the instructions below to extend your work permit from…

pageTitle: Situation in Iran: Temporary immigration measures for workers in Canada
heading: Iranian visitors and study permit holders
text: Iranian nationals in Canada who hold a visitor record or study permit can no longer apply for a different type of permit under special measures. You can apply to extend your current temporary resident status in Canada through the regular process. • Extend your stay as a visitor • Extend your study permit

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
text: These measures were updated on August 1, 2025. Temporary immigration measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents have been extended until July 31, 2026. Temporary immigration measures for Israeli nationals in Canada ended on July 31, 2025. • If you already applied and need to access your application, you can sign in to your IRCC secure account . • If you’d like to extend your stay in Canada, you can explore our regular immigration programs . Temporary immigration measures remain in place for • Palestinian passport holders (or holders of another travel document issued by the State of Palestine) who are in Canada as temporary residents • foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents where the foreign national family member is in Canada as a temporary resident • both the foreign national family member and the citizen or permanent resident left Palestine on or after October 7, 2023 A family member is defined as a • spouse or common-law partner • dependent child (yours, your spouse’s or your common-law partner’s) or • dependent child of a dependent child Tell us about your situation to find out what temporary measures you may be eligible for. Are you in Canada as a temporary resident (you have valid temporary resident status)? • Yes Are you a Palestinian passport or travel document holder? Yes Did you arrive in Canada under the special measures for extended family in Gaza or via the assisted departures out of Palestine? Yes • No Did you arrive in Canada under the special measures for extended family in Gaza? Yes • No Are you the family member of a Canadian citizen or permanent resident who left Palestine on or after October 7, 2023? Yes Did you leave…

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: Apply for temporary, limited health care coverage
text: You may be eligible to apply for the Interim Federal Health Program and get temporary, limited health care coverage. Find out if you’re eligible • you’re related to a Canadian citizen or permanent resident who lives or intends to live in Canada Based on your answers, you may be eligible to apply for a temporary resident visa (TRV) for extended family members in Gaza Find out if you’re eligible No special measures are available Based on your answers, there are no special measures for your situation. You can explore our immigration programs to see if there’s a program for you.

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: You chose study permit
text: If you meet all the requirements, you can apply for a study permit that will be valid for the length of your study program . The length of your study permit is also directly tied to the expiry of • the passport (or other travel document) that you used to come to Canada They must both be valid for the entire time you’re in Canada. Deadline: Submit your application on or before

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: Who can apply
text: To apply under the temporary measures, you must • be in Canada with valid temporary resident status when you apply • a decision is made on your application • meet eligibility and admissibility requirements Minor children may be able to study in Canada without a study permit but can still apply for one if they like. Learn more about studying in Canada as a minor .

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: Eligibility and admissibility requirements for a study permit
text: To be eligible to apply for a study permit, you must • provide a provincial attestation letter (PAL) or territorial attestation letter (TAL) from the province or territory where you plan to study, unless you meet one of the exceptions . • prove you have enough money to pay for your tuition fees • living expenses for you and the family members who came with you to Canada • transportation to return home for you and the family members who came with you to Canada • obey the law, have no criminal record and get a police certificate (if required) • be in good health and get a medical exam (if we ask you to) • prove to an officer that you will leave Canada at the end of your authorized stay

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: How to apply
text: You must apply online using the IRCC secure account. You can apply on paper if you meet one of the following exemptions: • You can’t apply online because of a disability. • There’s a barrier that prevents you from applying online or • makes applying online unreliable Contact us using our crisis web form if you can’t apply online. Complete all required fields in the form and include “ PSE2025 ” in the text box. Step 1: Get your documents ready Prepare the forms and supporting documents listed below. You need to upload them to your account when you apply. • Make sure you can open and view PDF forms. • If you have trouble downloading, saving or opening a form, visit our Help Centre (opens in a new tab) . • It’s easier to complete the forms on a computer. • You don’t need to print and sign the forms to upload them to your online application. You sign your online application electronically (opens in a new tab) . If you need help answering the questions in these forms, you can use our general instruction guide to apply for a study permit in Canada (opens in a new tab) : • Application to Change Conditions, Extend my Stay or Remain in Canada as a Student [IMM 5709] (opens in a new tab) • Use of a Representative [IMM 5476] (opens in a new tab) – (if applicable) Complete this form if somebody is applying for you, and you want them to be the main contact on your behalf. • Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual [IMM 5475] (opens in a new tab) – (if applicable) Complete this form if you want to allow us to release your personal information to someone else. This person will be able to get information on your case, for example, the status of your application. • Custodianship Declaration [IMM 5646] (opens in a new tab) – (if applicable) Complete this form…

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: After you apply
text: In most cases, you need to give your biometrics if you haven’t given them in the last 10 years. If you need to give them, we’ll send you a biometric instruction letter (BIL). Follow the instructions on your BIL. You might need to get a medical exam if you • plan to study in certain programs • plan to stay in Canada for more than 6 months If you need an exam, an officer will send you instructions on what to do. Find more information about medical exams . If you apply before your current status expires, you have maintained your status. Maintained status is a legal extension of status that allows a temporary resident to stay in Canada while we process their application. This means you can legally stay in Canada while you wait for a decision on your application. You must continue to meet the conditions described in your expiring document. We’ll mail the study permit to the Canadian address you gave us in your application. The study permit will outline all the conditions of your stay. If your mailing address changes before we approve your application, you must contact us to tell us your new address. While studying in Canada, you must • make progress toward completing your program • respect any conditions listed on your study permit • stop studying if you no longer meet the requirements Depending on your case, there may be conditions on your study permit such as • whether you’re allowed to work in Canada • whether you’re allowed to travel within Canada • the specific date you must leave Canada • where you can study (a specific DLI listed on your permit) You must change your DLI in your IRCC secure account before you can study at a different DLI. • whether you need an immigration medical examination

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: How to contact us
text: If you need to update your contact information or have questions, fill out the web form (opens in a new tab) : • Complete all required fields. • Include “ PSE2025 ” in the text box.

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: You chose study permit
text: If you meet all the requirements, you can apply for a study permit that will be valid for the length of your study program . The length of your study permit is also directly tied to the expiry of • the passport (or other travel document) that you used to come to Canada They must both be valid for the entire time you’re in Canada. Deadline: Submit your application on or before

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: Who can apply
text: To apply under the temporary measures, you must • be in Canada with valid temporary resident status when you apply • a decision is made on your application • meet eligibility and admissibility requirements Minor children may be able to study in Canada without a study permit but can still apply for one if they like. Learn more about studying in Canada as a minor .

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: Eligibility and admissibility requirements for a study permit
text: To be eligible to apply for a study permit, you must • provide a provincial attestation letter (PAL) or territorial attestation letter (TAL) from the province or territory where you plan to study, unless you meet one of the exceptions . • prove you have enough money to pay for your tuition fees • living expenses for you and the family members who came with you to Canada • transportation to return home for you and the family members who came with you to Canada • obey the law, have no criminal record and get a police certificate (if required) • be in good health and get a medical exam (if we ask you to) • prove to an officer that you will leave Canada at the end of your authorized stay

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: How to apply
text: You must apply online using the IRCC secure account. You can apply on paper if you meet one of the following exemptions: • You can’t apply online because of a disability. • There’s a barrier that prevents you from applying online or • makes applying online unreliable Contact us using our crisis web form if you can’t apply online. Complete all required fields in the form and include “ PSE2025 ” in the text box. Step 1: Get your documents ready Prepare the forms and supporting documents listed below. You need to upload them to your account when you apply. • Make sure you can open and view PDF forms. • If you have trouble downloading, saving or opening a form, visit our Help Centre (opens in a new tab) . • It’s easier to complete the forms on a computer. • You don’t need to print and sign the forms to upload them to your online application. You sign your online application electronically (opens in a new tab) . If you need help answering the questions in these forms, you can use our general instruction guide to apply for a study permit in Canada (opens in a new tab) : • Application to Change Conditions, Extend my Stay or Remain in Canada as a Student [IMM 5709] (opens in a new tab) • Use of a Representative [IMM 5476] (opens in a new tab) – (if applicable) Complete this form if somebody is applying for you, and you want them to be the main contact on your behalf. • Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual [IMM 5475] (opens in a new tab) – (if applicable) Complete this form if you want to allow us to release your personal information to someone else. This person will be able to get information on your case, for example, the status of your application. • Custodianship Declaration [IMM 5646] (opens in a new tab) – (if applicable) Complete this form…

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: After you apply
text: In most cases, you need to give your biometrics if you haven’t given them in the last 10 years. If you need to give them, we’ll send you a biometric instruction letter (BIL). Follow the instructions on your BIL. You might need to get a medical exam if you • plan to study in certain programs • plan to stay in Canada for more than 6 months If you need an exam, an officer will send you instructions on what to do. Find more information about medical exams . If you apply before your current status expires, you have maintained your status. Maintained status is a legal extension of status that allows a temporary resident to stay in Canada while we process their application. This means you can legally stay in Canada while you wait for a decision on your application. You must continue to meet the conditions described in your expiring document. We’ll mail the study permit to the Canadian address you gave us in your application. The study permit will outline all the conditions of your stay. If your mailing address changes before we approve your application, you must contact us to tell us your new address. While studying in Canada, you must • make progress toward completing your program • respect any conditions listed on your study permit • stop studying if you no longer meet the requirements Depending on your case, there may be conditions on your study permit such as • whether you’re allowed to work in Canada • whether you’re allowed to travel within Canada • the specific date you must leave Canada • where you can study (a specific DLI listed on your permit) You must change your DLI in your IRCC secure account before you can study at a different DLI. • whether you need an immigration medical examination

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: Eligibility and admissibility requirements for an open work permit
text: To be eligible to apply for an open work permit, you must • obey the law and have no record of criminal activity (we may ask you to give us a police clearance certificate ) • not be a danger to Canada’s security • be in good health and get a medical exam (if required to) • not plan to work for an employer listed with the status “ineligible” on the list of employers who failed to comply with the conditions • not plan to work for an employer that offers striptease, erotic dance, escort services or erotic massages on a regular basis • prove that you have enough money to pay for living expenses for you and the family members who came with you to Canada • transportation to return home for you and the family members who came with you to Canada • prove to an officer that you will leave Canada at the end of your authorized stay

pageTitle: Measures for Palestinian passport holders in Canada and foreign national family members of Canadian citizens and permanent residents in Canada
heading: How to apply
text: • Complete the paper application to change conditions, extend my stay or remain in Canada as a visitor or temporary resident permit holder (IMM 5708) (opens in a new tab) . • When completing form IMM 5708, under section 3 at the top of the form, make sure to select “Get a new temporary resident permit.” • Complete the document checklist (IMM 5557) (opens in a new tab) . • Provide a letter stating “I am exempt from paying fees under the PSE2025 in-Canada temporary measures.” • If you have a valid Palestinian passport or other travel document, provide a copy. • Put the completed forms and supporting documents in a 23 cm x 30.5 cm (9″ x 12″) envelope. Do not include a prepaid envelope with your application . • Address the envelope as follows: Case Processing Centre in Edmonton Immigration Refugees and Citizenship Canada c/o Visitor and Temporary Resident Permits, Station 303 9700 Jasper Avenue NW, Suite 55 Edmonton, AB T5J 4C3 • Write “ PSE2025 ” on the mailing envelope and on the first page of the application or covering document. Each family member must submit their own application; however, you can send all the documents to the Case Processing Centre in Edmonton in 1 envelope. Send us a web form (opens in a new tab) . • Complete all required fields. • Include “ PSE2025 ” in the text box.

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
heading: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
text: • Who was eligible to apply • Application process (Closed) All available spaces have been filled As of March 6, 2025, we received enough temporary resident visa (TRV) applications to fill the available spaces. We’re no longer • issuing unique reference codes • accepting new applications under these measures Find out what happens if you have an application in progress . Be careful of suspicious sources promising to help you leave Gaza! Scammers may use fake phone numbers that look like ours to ask you for money. These are not agents or consultants acting on our behalf. We’ll never ask you for payment by telephone. If someone claiming to be from IRCC requests payment this way, it's a scam! If you’re not sure if something is a scam, you can • learn more about fraud and how to protect yourself

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
heading: Documents to bring
text: Make sure you have the following documents with you when you travel to Canada: • valid passport (or other travel document) Your passport must have a valid temporary resident visa counterfoil or valid temporary resident permit counterfoil in it • certificate for the Interim Federal Health Program (IFHP) We send you this certificate after your application is approved. • It proves you’re eligible for temporary health care coverage in Canada. • copy of the decision letter you got when your application was approved The following documents are not required but may make your arrival in Canada easier: • marriage or divorce certificates • identification documents, such as a driver’s licence • adoption certificates for adopted children • immunization or vaccination records for children

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
heading: When you arrive at the airport in Canada
text: When you get to the airport, you need to verify your identity and complete your declaration. Depending on which airport you arrive at, this may be done • on screen using a primary inspection kiosk or eGate (opens in a new tab) or • with a border services officer from the Canada Border Services Agency When you speak to a border services officer, tell them that you came to Canada under the Gaza special measures. The officer will refer you for a secondary immigration examination. During the secondary immigration examination, a border services officer will help you finalize your entry into Canada. Make sure you get a temporary resident document from the border services officer. A temporary resident document is a document that details your immigration status in Canada. Depending on your situation, you’ll get a visitor record or a temporary resident permit. Your document will include • how long you can stay in Canada • the keyword “ MOME2023 ” under the remarks section Make sure your document has this keyword. It is used to identify you as somebody who came to Canada under the Gaza special measures and will allow you to get free settlement services in Canada . • If the keyword is missing, make sure you tell the Canada Border Services Agency officer before you leave the examination area. Ask them for a new temporary resident document that includes the “ MOME2023 ” keyword. • any conditions that you must follow while in Canada In most cases, your period of stay will be for up to 3 years, as long as your passport and biometrics are valid for the entire time. If your passport or biometrics expire in less than 3 years, your temporary resident document will expire at the same time. However, you can apply to extend your status before it expires.

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
heading: Apply for an open work permit or study permit, or to stay in Canada longer
text: Under temporary in-Canada measures, you may be eligible to apply • to extend your stay in Canada as a visitor (visitor record) • for an open work permit • to stay in Canada as a temporary resident permit holder Apply for a temporary resident permit only if you can’t apply to extend your status as a visitor. Find out if you’re eligible and how to apply.

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
heading: Temporary health care coverage
text: You’re eligible for free temporary health care coverage in Canada through the IFHP. After your application is approved, we’ll return your passport and give you an IFHP certificate at the same time. • You must have this certificate to get coverage under the IFHP. • You need to show it to health care providers to get eligible health products and services. • You and each family member that came with you should have your own certificate. If you don’t get an IFHP certificate when you get your passport back, you can contact us to request one . Coverage is valid for 3 months, starting on the date you enter Canada, regardless of the date on your certificate. This coverage includes basic, supplementary and prescription drug coverage and the cost of 1 immigration medical exam, if required. Learn more about the coverage and how to get products and services.

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
heading: Get free settlement services
text: While in Canada, you can get free settlement services from an IRCC-funded service provider organization. To get services, you must have the “ MOME2023 ” keyword under the “Remarks” section on your temporary resident document, such as your • temporary resident permit • study permit (if you applied for one) • open work permit (if you applied for one) If your temporary resident document doesn’t have the “MOME2023” keyword and you left the secure zone at the airport, you can contact us to request an updated document . Find out how to get free settlement services.

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
heading: Completing your application: Biometrics and admissibility
text: Before we can approve your application, we need to • collect your fingerprints and photo (biometrics) if you need to provide them • make sure you’re admissible to Canada If your application passes a preliminary eligibility and admissibility assessment, we’ll work with local authorities to advocate for your exit out of Gaza. The Government of Canada • doesn’t decide who can leave Gaza • can’t guarantee that you’ll be authorized to exit Gaza You should make an appointment with the Embassy of Canada in Cairo to finalize your application and submit your biometrics. You should make an appointment with the Embassy of Canada in Cairo to finalize your application and submit your biometrics. We need to collect your biometrics if you • are between the ages of 14 and 79 • haven’t given your biometrics in the last 10 years Given the situation, we understand that you may not be able to give your biometrics or complete a full admissibility check until you’ve left Gaza. If you’re still in Gaza, your biometric instruction letter will not expire after 30 days, and your application will not be affected. You don’t need to contact us to get a new letter or to ask for an extension. However, if you’re already outside of Gaza and the deadline in your letter has not yet passed, you should follow the instructions in your biometric instruction letter. After you submit your biometrics, we’ll complete your security screening to make sure you’re admissible to Canada.

heading: If you have questions or need to update the contact information on the application
text: If you have questions or need to update the address or other contact information after the application has been submitted, contact us using our crisis web form or by calling our dedicated phone line . How to use our crisis web form to send us a new address or new contact information: • When asked " What do you need to do? " select the option " I am affected by the crisis in Palestine. " • Fill out the form using the same information you gave us when you applied. • In the " Tell us about your situation " text box, give us your new address or contact information. How to use our crisis web form to send us a new address or new contact information: • When asked “ What do you need to do? ” select the option “I am affected by the crisis in Palestine.” • Fill out the form using the same information you gave us when you applied. • In the “ Tell us about your situation ” text box, give us your new address or contact information. Call us at +1-613-321-4243. • This phone number is available both in and outside Canada. • We accept charges for collect calls or calls with reverse charges. • Wait times may be longer due to call volumes. • Use the phone prompts to get to an agent. • If you are updating your contact information, give the agent your application information and tell the agent that you need to update your address or contact information. • Monday to Friday, 6:30 a.m. to 7 p.m. (ET) • Saturday and Sunday, closed Call us at +1-613-321-4243. • This phone number is available both in and outside Canada. • We accept charges for collect calls or calls with reverse charges. • Wait times may be longer due to call volumes. • Use the phone prompts to get to an agent. • If you are updating your contact information, give the agent your application information and tell the agent that you…

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
heading: Eligibility
text: There were 2 groups who were eligible to apply for a temporary resident visa (TRV) under these special measures. Eligibility under group 1 • have a passport or travel document issued by the State of Palestine • be living in the Gaza Strip on the day your application is submitted • have an eligible anchor • be the anchor’s spouse or common-law partner • sibling (half-siblings and adopted siblings included) • not be inadmissible to Canada for reasons other than financial ones Eligibilityle under group 2 • be the family member of somebody described in group 1 Per the Immigration and Refugee Protection Regulations, family member is defined as a spouse or common-law partner • dependent child (yours, your spouse’s or your common-law partner’s) • dependent child of a dependent child • not be inadmissible to Canada for reasons other than financial ones

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Extended family
heading: Other requirements
text: You must have an immigration medical exam (IME) done if • you’ve been in any of these designated countries or territories for 6 months in a row or longer and • you were there within the year before you enter Canada The same applies to the family members who are coming with you to Canada. If you need to get an IME, it can be done outside of Gaza. Find out more about getting an IME. Your anchor will support you during your application and once you arrive in Canada. Make sure your anchor meets the following requirements. • be a Canadian citizen or permanent resident • be 18 years of age or older • be related to the person described under group 1 • live or intend to live in Canada • be willing to support you and your family members for 1 year to help you settle and integrate in Canada • accept payment from you or your family members in exchange for being your anchor • be receiving social assistance for a reason other than disability • have an ongoing bankruptcy • be in default of any sponsorship undertaking, support payment obligation or debt owed to the Government of Canada • be convicted of a serious crime • be subject to a removal order in Canada An eligible Canadian citizen or permanent resident can be the anchor for more than one person described in group 1, as long as they agree to support that person and the eligible family members who come with them through these measures. To confirm they intend to support you and your family members, the anchor must complete the statutory declaration form (IMM 5992) (opens in a new tab) stating they intend to • meet you at the airport and get you to where you’re staying • help you find temporary and permanent housing • make sure you have food, clothes and other basic needs • introduce you to life in Canada (for example, public…

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Special measures for extended family
heading: If you submitted a TRV application under these measures
text: We’re processing submitted TRV applications in the order we received them. • If you submitted a TRV application before all spaces were filled , we’ll continue to process it under the special measures. • If you submitted a TRV application but there were no more spaces available , we’ll email you about your application. Make sure you check your email regularly, including your junk or spam folders. We’re refunding temporary resident visa application fees and biometrics fees for eligible Palestinian nationals and their family members who applied to come to Canada under the Gaza special measures for extended family. Find out who’s eligible for a refund and how to get one .

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Special measures for extended family
heading: If you requested a unique reference code through our crisis web form, but you didn’t submit a TRV application
text: Requesting a unique code is not the same as submitting a TRV application. We’re no longer accepting new TRV applications under these measures. If you did not submit a TRV application before all available spaces were filled, you won’t be able to submit one under these measures. If you’re interested in applying to come to Canada, explore our other temporary or permanent resident immigration programs to see if there’s a program for you.

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Settlement services in Canada
heading: Who can get free settlement services
text: You can get free settlement services in Canada if you’re outside of Quebec and you meet the requirements for 1 of the groups below: • You hold a passport or another travel document issued by the State of Palestine, and you left Gaza on or after September 1, 2023 • arrived in Canada by December 31, 2026 updated • are in Canada with valid temporary resident status • You’re the immediate family member of someone under group (1), and you arrived in Canada by December 31, 2026 updated • are in Canada with valid temporary resident status • You were part of the Government of Canada assisted departures out of Gaza or the West Bank between November 1, 2023, and December 31, 2024, and you are in Canada with valid temporary resident status. Free settlement services are available until March 31, 2027. An immediate family member is defined as a • spouse or common-law partner • dependent child (yours, your spouse’s or your common-law partner’s) or • dependent child of a dependent child Contact one of Quebec’s partner community organizations to find out if you’re eligible for free settlement services.

pageTitle: Crisis in Gaza: Settlement services in Canada
heading: How to get free settlement services
text: To access free settlement services, you must have a valid temporary resident document with the “MOME2023” keyword in the remarks section at the bottom of the document. • If you don’t have a temporary resident document with the “MOME2023” keyword, you can contact us to request one . To get services, find a service provider organization in your area and bring your temporary resident document with you to show the service provider. Find a service provider organization • Temporary health-care coverage

pageTitle: Settling in Canada
heading: Settling in Canada
text: Settling in a new country can be an exciting, but challenging experience. Our free services are here to help you transition smoothly to everyday life in Canada.

pageTitle: Settling in Canada

text: Starting April 1, 2026, economic class permanent residents can only access newcomer services for a limited time. • the changes to the eligibility period • why we made the changes Permanent residents can get help from settlement service providers for free. Get free in-person and online services to help you prepare for life in Canada before you arrive . Get resources to help you improve your English or French. Learn about driver’s licenses, road rules and car insurance. Learn about your first tax year, benefits and credits. Resources for newcomer women Resources for newcomer youth

pageTitle: Settling in Canada
heading: Features

Get your PR card or PR travel document and manage your status as a permanent resident. Learn about common scams aimed at newcomers and the ways to recognize, avoid and report them.

pageTitle: Understand permanent resident status
heading: Understand permanent resident status
text: A permanent resident (PR) is someone who has been given PR status by immigrating to Canada , but is not a Canadian citizen . PRs are usually citizens of other countries, while some may be stateless persons. When refugees resettle in Canada from overseas , they become PRs through the Government-Assisted Refugee Program or the Private Sponsorship of Refugees Program . If you make a refugee claim in Canada, you don’t become a PR immediately. To become one, the Immigration and Refugee Board must first approve your claim. Then, you must apply for and obtain PR status. You’re not a PR if you’re only in Canada for a short time (for example, as a student or foreign worker).

pageTitle: Understand permanent resident status
heading: Confirmation of Permanent Residence document
text: If we approve your application for permanent residence while you’re outside Canada, we’ll send you • a Confirmation of Permanent Residence (COPR) • a permanent resident visa (if you’re from a country that needs a visa) If we approve your application while you’re in Canada, we’ll upload your electronic Confirmation of Permanent Residence (e-COPR) to your account in the Permanent Residence Portal. You can use your COPR or e-COPR to apply for government services while you wait to receive your permanent resident card (PR card). Learn more about the COPR .

pageTitle: Understand permanent resident status
heading: The PR card
text: Your PR card proves that you have PR status in Canada. If you travel outside Canada and return on a commercial vehicle (like an airplane, boat, train or bus), you must show • your passport or refugee travel document If you’re traveling outside Canada without a valid PR card with you, apply for a permanent resident travel document (PRTD) before returning to Canada by commercial vehicle. If your PR card was lost, stolen or damaged , apply for a replacement PR card . How to properly dispose of your expired PR card Destroy your old PR card by cutting it in half before disposing of it so you protect your information and privacy.

pageTitle: Understand permanent resident status
heading: What permanent residents can do
text: • get most social benefits that Canadian citizens receive, including a social insurance number to work in Canada, and health care coverage (check your provincial website for more information) • can live, work or study anywhere in Canada • can apply for Canadian citizenship • get protection under Canadian law and the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms • must pay taxes and respect all Canadian laws at the federal, provincial and municipal levels

pageTitle: Understand permanent resident status
heading: What permanent residents cannot do
text: You’re not allowed to • vote or run for political office or • hold some jobs that need a high-level security clearance

pageTitle: Understand permanent resident status
heading: Time lived in Canada
text: To keep your PR status, you must have been in Canada for at least 730 days during the last five years. These 730 days don’t need to be continuous. Some of your time outside Canada may count towards the 730 days you need. • Find out if your time outside Canada counts toward your 730 days . • Track your time in Canada with our travel journal .

pageTitle: Understand permanent resident status
heading: Losing your permanent resident status
text: You won’t lose your PR status when your PR card expires. You will only lose your PR status if • an officer determines you are no longer a PR after an inquiry or PRTD appeal following a refusal • you voluntarily renounce your PR status • a removal order made against you comes into force • you become a Canadian citizen Even if you don't meet the residency obligation, you will stay a PR until an official decision is made on your status. Learn how to avoid travel delays if you are not sure of your status .

pageTitle: Understand permanent resident status
heading: Voluntarily giving up (renouncing) permanent resident status
text: We won’t change your PR status automatically and we’ll always inform you of the change. In some cases, you may decide you don’t want to be a PR of Canada anymore. For example, you may want to renounce your PR status in the following situations: • You have not met your residency obligations by being outside of Canada for a long period of time. • You plan to visit Canada and want to avoid the processing delays from a formal assessment of your PR status when you arrive. In these cases, you may not be able to enter Canada until your PR status is resolved either by applying for a PRTD or by voluntarily giving up (renouncing) your PR status. Find out how to voluntarily renounce your permanent resident status .

pageTitle: Understand permanent resident status
heading: Features
text: • Becoming a Canadian citizen • Permanent resident card • Permanent resident status on humanitarian and compassionate grounds The Canoo app provides access to many cultural sites and events across Canada. As a new permanent resident, you can get a free 1-year membership. Learn more about the Canoo mobile app (opens in a new tab)

pageTitle: Apply urgently for a permanent resident card or permanent resident travel document
heading: Apply urgently for a permanent resident card or permanent resident travel document
text: The Canada Post labour disruption will cause delays in mail delivery We’re still processing applications normally. However, there will be delays in • sending applications mailed within or to Canada We encourage you to apply online if possible . If you need to apply on paper, use another courier to send us your application. Depending on your situation, you may be eligible to apply urgently for either • a permanent resident (PR) card or • a permanent resident travel document (PRTD) Answer the questions below to find out if you’re eligible.

pageTitle: Apply urgently for a permanent resident card or permanent resident travel document
heading: Tell us about your situation
text: Do you urgently need a PR card or PRTD for one of these reasons ? • Yes I’m... leaving Canada in less than 3 weeks Select the option that applies to you: I haven’t applied for a PR card yet. • I already applied for a PR card but won’t get it before I need to leave • I can’t find my PR card. • leaving Canada in more than 3 weeks Select the option that applies to you: I haven’t applied for a PR card yet. • I recently applied for a PR card. • I can’t find my PR card. • outside Canada Select the option that applies to you: I don’t have my valid PR card with me. • My PR card expired while I was travelling. • My PR card was lost or stolen while I was travelling.

pageTitle: Apply urgently for a permanent resident card or permanent resident travel document
heading: Eligible reasons for urgent processing
text: To be eligible for urgent processing , you must show that your need the PR card or PRTD to travel for one of these reasons: • work related to your current job • your own serious illness • the serious illness or death of a family member • the loss or theft of your PR card while you were temporarily outside Canada • a crisis, emergency or vulnerable situation you are in • other reasons that an officer may deem legitimate (for PRTD applications only)

pageTitle: Apply urgently for a permanent resident card or permanent resident travel document
heading: I’m leaving Canada in less than 3 weeks, and I haven’t applied for a PR card yet.
text: We can’t process a PR card application in less than 3 weeks. However, you can apply urgently for a PRTD once • you’re outside Canada and • your flight back to Canada is leaving within the next 5 days A PRTD lets you re-enter Canada if you don’t have a valid PR card. Without a PR card or a PRTD, you can travel to Canada only by land using a private vehicle , like a car. • Prepare your PRTD application (opens in a new tab) . • Include all these documents with your application: proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary • a copy of your ticket • a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date • a letter that explains the reason for the urgency • proof of the urgency, such as a doctor’s note • letter from your employer • if applicable, English or French translations of your documents • Check that your documents are clear and easy to read . If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application. • Tell us that your application is urgent. If you’re applying online , check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal. • If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope. We’ll review your application. If you forget to include proof of urgency, or if your situation doesn’t qualify for urgent processing, we’ll process your application normally. • Find out what happens after you apply for a PRTD (opens in a new tab) . • When you return to Canada, apply for your PR card (opens in a new tab) . Even if you’re eligible for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your PR card or travel document on time . • You can check the current processing times for PR cards . • We don’t currently post processing times for PRTDs. However, we prioritize the…

pageTitle: Apply urgently for a permanent resident card or permanent resident travel document
heading: I’m leaving Canada in less than 3 weeks, and I already applied for a PR card but won’t get it before I need to leave.
text: We can’t process a PR card application in less than 3 weeks. However, you can apply urgently for a PRTD once • you’re outside Canada and • your flight back to Canada is leaving within the next 5 days A PRTD lets you re-enter Canada if you don’t have a valid PR card. Without a PR card or a PRTD, you can travel to Canada only by land using a private vehicle, like a car. • Prepare your PRTD application (opens in a new tab) . • Include all these documents with your application: proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary • a copy of your ticket • a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date • a letter that explains the reason for the urgency • proof of the urgency, such as a doctor’s note • letter from your employer • if applicable, English or French translations of your documents • Check that your documents are clear and easy to read . If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application. • Tell us that your application is urgent. If you’re applying online, check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal. • If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope. We’ll review your application. If you forget to include proof of urgency, or if your situation doesn’t qualify for urgent processing, we’ll process your application normally. Find out what happens after you apply for a PRTD (opens in a new tab) . Even if you’re eligible for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your PR card or travel document on time . • You can check the current processing times for PR cards . • We don’t currently post processing times for PRTDs. However, we prioritize the processing of these applications.

If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application. • Tell us that your application is urgent.
