heading: Closed
text: As of April 17, 2025, we are assessing the applications already received to confirm if there are enough to fill the available spaces. We’re assessing the applications submitted in the order they were submitted, and applications received in excess of the cap will be returned to applicants. We’ll post more information on this page if we start to accept applications again for this pathway. Once we’ve approved your application you can start the process of settling in Canada. Your next steps will depend on where in the world you are.

pageTitle: Closed: Family-based permanent residence pathway for people affected by the conflict in Sudan
heading: Make sure you keep your information up to date
text: Use the web form if you need to update your contact information or tell us about changes to your family situation (for example, marriage, birth, divorce or death).

pageTitle: Closed: Family-based permanent residence pathway for people affected by the conflict in Sudan
heading: If you are outside of Canada
text: Before you come to Canada, you’ll be eligible for free pre-arrival settlement services to help you learn about living and working in Canada. These services • include information about life in Canada • include an employment skills assessment • will refer you to resources to help you start your new life in Canada See our fact sheets for more information about settling in Canada .

pageTitle: Closed: Family-based permanent residence pathway for people affected by the conflict in Sudan
heading: If you are in Canada
text: There are many free services in Canada to help you or your family members • learn or improve English and French • find a job (if you don’t have one already) • find activities in your new community Find out about services in your area . You can also learn more about starting your life in Canada . We’ll mail your permanent resident (PR) card to your address in Canada. If you didn’t give us your Canadian mailing address when you became a permanent resident • use our online address notification tool to send it to us • you must give us your address within 180 days of the day you become a permanent resident If you don’t send it within 180 days, we’ll cancel the PR card and you’ll have to apply for another one (and pay new processing fees).

pageTitle: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
heading: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
text: Follow these steps to avoid problems or delays when you bring foreign business guests to Canada:

pageTitle: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
heading: Step 1: Carry out your responsibilities
text: • Perform due diligence checks on your potential business partner. Before you enter into any formal partnership, you should investigate your potential partner’s business track record, assets and liabilities, reputation and current legal status. This can protect your company’s interests and reputation. It will also help you decide if aspects of your potential partner’s background would cause problems if you were to bring that person to Canada for business. Please see the Trade Commissioner Service for advice. • Liaise with the Canadian trade commissioner responsible for the sector and country your visitor is coming from. • Provide your potential visiting business partner with 2 copies of a letter of invitation . • Provide your potential visiting business partner with 24-hour contact information for a representative from your business. Your visiting partner may be asked to provide the information to the border services officer when arriving in Canada, or • need to contact you due to travel delays

pageTitle: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
heading: Step 2: Register your international event with us and get certain support services (if applicable)
text: • You can get useful information and support on Canada’s entry and visa requirements from our Special Events Unit. • Find out more about how to get support to organize an international event in Canada .

pageTitle: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
heading: Step 3: Find out if the person you’re inviting needs a visa or an electronic travel authorization (eTA)
text: Answer a few questions to find out what your participant may need to apply for, and what documents they’ll need to come to Canada. They will also need to • carry proper travel documents and identification for themselves and any children travelling with them • convince an immigration officer that they satisfy all other requirements to enter Canada , the participant may not be allowed to enter the country

pageTitle: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
heading: Step 4: Prepare a letter of invitation for the person you are inviting
text: • Find out what to include in a letter of invitation to a business visitor. • If your event is registered with IRCC, include the event code in your letter of invitation. Your participants should include the event code in their visa applications to identify themselves as event participants .

pageTitle: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
heading: Step 5: Encourage your participants to apply as early as possible and before booking their flights
text: If your visitor needs an eTA (or is eligible for one), they must apply online before travelling to Canada by air. It’s best for travellers to apply for their eTA before booking their flight to Canada. In most cases, applicants will receive an approved eTA within minutes of applying. However, some requests may take several days to process. If this is the case, applicants can expect an email from us within 72 hours of applying that tells them what the next steps are. If your visitor needs a visa to visit Canada, they must apply for a visitor visa just like any other temporary visitor to Canada. There is no separate application for business visitors . The visitor visa covers all visitors, including those coming to Canada on business. If they’re applying for the first time , your visitor needs to give their fingerprints and photo (biometrics ), unless they’re exempt. If the visitor gave biometrics before, they ’re probably still valid. If so, they don’t have to give them again. They can check when their biometrics expire by using the check status tool . We issue multiple- entry visas whenever possible because it makes it easier for business partners or contacts who need to make repeat visits to Canada. However, there may be circumstances when only a single-entry visa is issued. Your visitor’s application may be delayed or refused if it’s not complete, or if required documents are missing or suspect. For example, applicants will need to give their fingerprints and photo (biometrics) to support their visitor visa application. Check application processing times to find out how long it takes to process most complete applications.

pageTitle: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
heading: Step 6: Consider these things when organizing an international event or a meeting in Canada
text: If you invite high-level government officials such as heads of state, ministers or diplomats to your event and you have questions about protocol and reception services, contact the Office of Protocol at Global Affairs Canada. For more information, visit the Office of Protocol’s website . You may consider setting up a reception counter at the airport, where your representatives can greet delegates. Contact the airport in advance if you plan to do so. All equipment and goods brought into Canada for the event must be reported to the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA). Contact the CBSA for information on border requirements for • delegates and goods entering Canada • documents and procedures that may be needed Contact the nearest CBSA office in your province or area, or visit the CBSA International Events website .

pageTitle: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
heading: Minors travelling with business guests
text: Canada has laws and regulations to protect children. Delegates who plan to travel with a child (17 years of age or younger) should consult the entry requirements for minors .

pageTitle: Event organizers and Canadian businesses - bring business guests to Canada
heading: What to do if an eTA or a visitor visa is refused
text: If your potential partner’s eTA or business visa is refused, please see How do I get help if my application is refused ? If there is new or additional information that should be considered, your partner can re apply and submit this new information. There is no minimum amount of time to wait before they can apply again.

pageTitle: Visitor visas for workers and students: How to apply from inside Canada
heading: You need to give your biometrics
text: In most cases, you now need to give your fingerprints and photo (biometrics) after you apply. Make sure you follow all the steps to give your biometrics . Are you a student or worker who received a V-1 visa? This is not an error. Find out more about the V-1 visa for students and workers. A work permit or study permit is not a visa. • We won’t automatically issue you a visitor visa if you extend your work or study permit. • If you need a new visitor visa, you will need to submit a separate application.

pageTitle: Visitor visas for workers and students: How to apply from inside Canada
heading: Who can apply for a visitor visa from inside Canada
text: You can only apply for a visitor visa from inside Canada if you meet all of these criteria: • already are in Canada • hold a valid study or work permit • want to leave and return to Canada in the near future, and • have an existing visitor visa that’s expired, or is about to expire, or that was valid for only 1 entry You must follow the process to apply for a visa from outside Canada instead of the inside Canada process outlined below. There are different requirements you may need to meet since you’re outside Canada. • apply at least 2 months before you plan to leave Canada • not leave Canada until you get your visitor visa You may be subject to • additional requirements, like biometrics (if not already on file) and/or a medical exam • a longer processing time • a delay in your return to Canada You’ll only be able to travel back to Canada once you get your new visitor visa in your passport. There’s no guarantee that you’ll be approved. Complete this web form before you leave Canada . • We may need to transfer your file to one of our offices abroad. • We’ll also need to determine if you meet all the visa requirements for an applicant outside Canada. • If you don’t contact us, there could be a processing delay or your passport could be returned without a visa. If we approve your application, we send you a message in your online account and mail you your new work or study permit. After we approve your application to extend your permit As soon as we approve your application to extend your work or study permit, you can apply for your visitor visa (even before you get your new work or study permit in the mail). However, you must provide proof that we approved your application, such as: • a screenshot of the approval message we sent to your online account • your application…

pageTitle: Visitor visas for workers and students: How to apply from inside Canada
heading: How to apply for your new visitor visa
text: You need to apply online in your IRCC secure account. If you don’t already have an account, follow these steps to register . You’ll need to select “Apply to Come to Canada” from your account main page to get started. To get the right application form, provide these answers in the online questionnaire: • For the first question “What would you like to do in Canada?”, select “Study” if you have a valid study permit or • “Work” if you have a valid work permit • When asked “What is your current country/territory of residence?”, select “Canada”. • Answer the questions on the next pages about your work or studies. • You may be given the option to extend your current study or work permit or to apply for a “Temporary Resident Visa”. Make sure you select “Temporary Resident Visa”. The application form listed in your document checklist will be called Application for Visitor Visa (Temporary Resident Visa) Made Outside of Canada (IMM 5257) . This is the form you need to fill out, even when you apply from inside Canada. Use your account to pay your fees and check your application status. If you can’t apply online because of a disability, an exceptional circumstance beyond your control or a specific situation that is not included in the IRCC secure account questionnaire, such as travelling with an alien’s passport, you can apply on paper .

pageTitle: Business visitors attending meetings, events and conferences in Canada
heading: Step 1: Find out if you’re a business visitor or if you need to apply for a work permit instead
text: Business visitors are persons who stay in Canada for a few days or a few weeks to attend meetings or an event, and can stay for up to 6 months. To qualify as a business visitor to Canada, you must show that • you plan to stay for less than 6 months • you don’t plan to enter the Canadian labour market • your main place of business, and source of income and profits is outside Canada • you have documents that support your application • you meet Canada’s basic entry requirements, because you have a valid travel document, such as a passport • have enough money for your stay and to return home • plan to leave Canada at the end of your visit • are not a criminal, security or health risk to Canadians If you plan to stay for more than 6 months or plan to work in Canada, you may be considered a temporary worker and have to apply for a work permit . Activities you may conduct as a business visitor include • buying Canadian goods or services for a foreign business or government • taking orders for goods or services • going to meetings, conferences, conventions or trade fairs • giving after-sales service as part of a warranty or sales agreement • being trained by a Canadian parent company that you work for outside Canada • training employees of a Canadian branch of a foreign company • being trained by a Canadian company that has sold you equipment or services Under the Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement, a U S or Mexican national may also take part in other activities, such as research, marketing and general services. For more information, see the Global Affairs Canada website .

pageTitle: Business visitors attending meetings, events and conferences in Canada
heading: Step 2: Find out if you need a visa or an electronic travel authorization (eTA) to travel to Canada
text: If you qualify as a business visitor, answer a few questions to find out what you may need to apply for, and which documents you’ll need to come to Canada.

pageTitle: Business visitors attending meetings, events and conferences in Canada
heading: Step 3: Check with your meeting, event or conference organizer to get an event code (if applicable)
text: If you need a visitor visa to travel to Canada and your meeting, event or conference is registered with Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC), your organizer will provide you with an event code and instructions about how to apply for a visa. • Entering the event code on your visa application form helps identify you as a participant of an event registered with us.

pageTitle: Business visitors attending meetings, events and conferences in Canada
heading: Step 5: Find out what you need to bring when you travel to Canada
text: Make sure that you have the following documents when you arrive at the border (don’t pack them in your suitcase): • a passport or travel document that is valid for your entire stay • a valid visitor visa, if applicable • if you need an eTA, the same passport you used in your application • if you’re a lawful permanent resident of the United States (US), a valid green card (or equivalent official proof of status in the U S ) and a valid passport from your country of nationality (or an equivalent document) • letters of support from your parent company, and a letter of invitation from the Canadian host business or a letter of recognition from the Canada Border Services Agency • other documents such as warranty or service agreements, or contracts, if relevant to your visit • 24-hour contact details of your business host in Canada • proof that you have enough money to cover both your stay in Canada and your return home

pageTitle: Business visitors attending meetings, events and conferences in Canada
heading: Minors travelling with you to Canada
text: Canada has laws and regulations to protect children. Delegates who plan to travel with a child (17 years of age or younger) should consult the entry requirements for minors .

pageTitle: Extend your stay in Canada (visitor record)
heading: Extend your stay in Canada (visitor record)
text: If you want to extend your stay in Canada as a visitor (stay in Canada longer), you need to apply for a visitor record . A visitor record is not a visa .

pageTitle: Extend your stay in Canada (visitor record)
heading: Image of a visitor record
text: From Error loading fee. Check the fee list . Error loading the number of days. Try again.

pageTitle: Extend your stay in Canada (visitor record)
heading: Processing times PR
text: Processing times will vary based on • the type of application • if the application is complete • how quickly we expect to process applications we’ve already received • how easily we can verify your information • how long you take to respond to any requests or concerns Your processing time may also be affected if more people apply to immigrate to Canada than we can bring in each year. Learn how processing times are calculated .

pageTitle: Electronic travel authorization
heading: Electronic travel authorization
text: This is the Government of Canada’s official web page to apply for an electronic travel authorization (eTA) to travel to, or transit through, Canada. It only costs CAN$7 . Find out what it is and how to apply online. The Government of Canada will perform updates to its online system from 12:00 am to 5:30 am ET on June 9, 2026 . The eTA application form will not be available at that time. We apologize for the inconvenience. To apply for an eTA, please return after 5:30 am ET on June 9 . The eTA system is currently down. We are working to resolve the issue. Thank you for your patience. The eTA system is experiencing delays. We are working to resolve this issue. Thank you for your patience.

pageTitle: Electronic travel authorization
heading: Most requested
text: • Find out what you need to travel to Canada • I’m visiting the US and want to visit Canada. Do I need an eTA?

pageTitle: Electronic travel authorization
heading: Services and information
text: العربية , 廣東話(繁體字) , 普通話(繁體字) , Български , English, Español , Filipino, Français , Deutsch , Ελληνικά , Magyarul , Italiano , 日本語 , 한국어 , Polski , Português , Português (Brasil) , ภาษาไทย To apply, you must have a valid passport, a credit or debit card and an email address, and you must answer a few questions in the online application. What to expect if we need more details from you, and things to keep in mind after you receive an eTA How to apply for a passport, and why you need a passport and not an eTA for travel to or through Canada Get online updates on pending applications as well as information on the status of your current eTA. Answers to common issues when you apply for an eTA

pageTitle: Super visa for parents and grandparents
heading: Super visa for parents and grandparents
text: You can visit your children or grandchildren (if they’re a permanent resident, a Canadian citizen or a registered Indian) for 5 years at a time with a super visa. It provides multiple entries for a period of up to 10 years. You can’t include dependants in this application. If you want to stay for 6 months or less, apply for a visitor visa .

pageTitle: Super visa for parents and grandparents
heading: Processing times
text: Check processing times You may need to give biometrics with your application. This processing time doesn’t include the time you need to give biometrics. Processing times will vary based on: • the type of application submitted • if the application is complete • how quickly we expect to process applications we’ve already received • how easily we can verify your information • how long you take to respond to any requests or concerns More people want to immigrate to Canada than we can bring in each year. Applications received after we meet our yearly limit may have to wait longer to be processed. Learn how processing times are calculated .

pageTitle: Transit through Canada
heading: Transit through Canada
text: You may need to transit through Canada on your way to another country. The document you need to transit through Canada depends on: • how you will travel through Canada (by air, car, bus, train, boat, including cruise ship) • how long it’ll take you to transit through Canada • the nationality of your passport • other factors, such as whether you are a permanent resident of the United States or a US visa holder To find out if you need a visa, you can • answer a few questions, or • review the options below Find out if you need a visa

pageTitle: Transit through Canada
heading: Visitor visa
text: You need a visitor visa if you’re from a visa-required country and you plan to: • visit Canada (even if you’re travelling by air and it’s for less than 48 hours) • stay in Canada more than 48 hours while transiting You also need a visitor visa if you’re from a visa-required country and you’re crossing the border by: Apply for a visitor visa

pageTitle: Transit through Canada
heading: Transit visa
text: You need a transit visa if you’re from a visa-required country and: • your international flight stops at a Canadian airport on its way to another country • you’ll be connecting between 2 international flights at one or more Canadian airports • your transit through Canada will last 48 hours or less • you don’t have a valid visitor visa • you’re not eligible for an eTA . Apply for a transit visa

pageTitle: Transit through Canada
heading: eTA
text: You need an eTA to transit through Canada by air if you’re from an eTA-required country . You don’t need an eTA if you’re transiting through Canada and you enter by train, bus, boat or cruise ship. You do need to bring the right travel documents .

pageTitle: Transit through Canada
heading: Transit without a visa
text: US citizens and lawful permanent residents of the U.S. can transit through Canada without a visa by: You just need to bring the right travel documents . You may be able to transit without a visa if you hold a passport from one of the following countries or territories and you meet certain conditions: • Taiwan (holder of passport without an personal identification number) Who can transit without a visa

pageTitle: Visitor record
heading: Visitor record
text: • How to apply If you can’t apply online • Travelling outside Canada You must apply online for your visitor record to extend your stay as a • worker authorized to work without a work permit or • student authorized to study without a study permit If you plan to travel outside Canada or the United States You must meet our entry requirements to return to Canada .

pageTitle: Visitor record
heading: Applying online
text: When you apply online, we get your application instantly, which can help us process your application faster. If you already applied on paper, you don’t have to resubmit your application online. To apply online, you’ll need • a scanner or camera to create electronic copies of your documents • a valid credit or debit card We accept credit cards and prepaid cards from: • UnionPay ® (for online applications only; debit cards not accepted) If you use a prepaid credit card, keep it for at least 18 months after you pay to make refunds easier. Find more on prepaid cards . We also accept all Visa Debit cards. The card you use doesn't have to be in your name. The cardholder’s name will appear on the receipt but it does not need to match your application. • be from a Canadian bank using INTERAC ® Online , and • be registered for online banking through your bank’s website. We also accept all Debit MasterCard ® and Visa ® Debit cards. If your card has the Visa Debit logo on it, alone or with the INTERAC ® Online logo, select Visa Debit as your payment method. The card you use does not have to be in your name. The cardholder’s name will appear on the receipt but it does not need to match your application. Even if you apply online, you should read the instruction guide before you complete your application. The guide will explain how to complete each field on the form. Venezuelan passport holders You may need to complete extra steps when you fill out your application . If the printed expiry date on your Venezuelan passport has passed If the printed expiry date has passed and you’re applying for a visitor visa, study permit, work permit or to extend your stay as a temporary resident in Canada, follow these steps: • Add 5 years to the printed expiry date shown on your passport and enter…

pageTitle: Visitor record
heading: If you can’t apply online
text: You can submit a paper application in any of these situations: • You can’t apply online because of a disability. • There’s a problem with the online application. What to do if you can’t apply online

pageTitle: Visitor visa (temporary resident visa)
heading: Visitor visa (temporary resident visa)
text: A visitor visa (also called a temporary resident visa) is an official document that we stick in your passport. It shows that you meet the requirements needed to travel to Canada. Some citizens of Indonesia and Malaysia are now eligible to apply for an eTA As of May 26, 2026, citizens of Indonesia and Malaysia who meet certain requirements may be eligible to apply for an eTA instead of a visitor visa, to travel to Canada by air. If you already have a valid visitor visa, you can use that to travel to Canada until it expires. Find out what you need to travel to Canada .

pageTitle: Visitor visa (temporary resident visa)
heading: Processing times
text: Check processing times You may need to give biometrics with your application. This processing time doesn’t include the time you need to give biometrics.

pageTitle: Visitor visa (temporary resident visa)
heading: Processing times
text: Processing times will vary based on: • the type of application submitted • if the application is complete • how quickly we expect to process applications we’ve already received • how easily we can verify your information • how long you take to respond to any requests or concerns More people want to immigrate to Canada than we can bring in each year. Applications received after we meet our yearly limit may have to wait longer to be processed. Learn how processing times are calculated .

pageTitle: Visitor visa (temporary resident visa)
heading: Features
text: If you’re using an immigration agent (consultant, lawyer or Quebec notary), they must be licensed to represent you or give advice.

pageTitle: Work in Canada temporarily
heading: Types of work permits
text: • Job offer not required Answer a few questions about yourself to find out • if there are special instructions Find the work permit you need.

pageTitle: Work in Canada temporarily
heading: Applications for work permits with special instructions
text: Applications for some open work permits and employer-specific work permits have special instructions:

pageTitle: Employers who have been found non-compliant
heading: Employers who have been found non-compliant
text: Employers who hire temporary workers may be inspected to make sure they meet their responsibilities as an employer under the Temporary Foreign Worker Program or International Mobility Program . If an employer is found non-compliant, they can receive either or both of the following: • a ban from hiring temporary workers (these employers are ineligible to hire temporary workers for a period of time)

pageTitle: Employers who have been found non-compliant
heading: Table footnotes
text: Return to footnote * Referrer There is no reason indicated because the employer was found non-compliant for an incident that occurred before new regulations came into effect on December 1, 2015.

pageTitle: Employers who have been found non-compliant
heading: Reasons employers may be found non-compliant
text: Employers may be found non-compliant for one or more of the following reasons: • The employer couldn’t show that the information they listed in the offer of employment was true, for a period of 6 years, starting on the first day the foreign national worked for them. Provision: 209.2(1)(b)(i) • The employer didn’t keep documents that showed they met the conditions of employing a foreign national, for a period of 6 years, starting on the first day the foreign national worked for them. Provision: 209.2(1)(b)(ii) or 209.3(1)(c)(ii) • The employer didn't have the money to pay the wages agreed to with a live-in caregiver. Provision: 209.3(1)(a)(iii)(C) • The employer couldn’t show that the description they gave for the job on the Labour Market Impact Assessment application was true, for a period of 6 years, starting on the first day the foreign national worked for them. Provision: 209.3(1)(c)(i) • The employer didn't show up for a meeting with the inspector, to answer questions and give documents the inspector asked for. Provision: 209.4(1)(a) • The employer didn't give the inspector the documents they asked for. Provision: 209.4(1)(b) • The employer didn't show up for an inspection, and didn't help or give information to the inspector when asked to. Provision: 209.4(1)(c) • The employer broke federal, provincial or territorial laws for hiring and recruiting employees in the province or territory where the foreign national worked. Provision: 209.2(1)(a)(ii) or 209.3(1)(a)(ii) • The pay or working conditions didn't match, or were not better than, what was listed on the offer of employment, or the job was not the same as what was listed on the offer of employment. Provision: 209.2(1)(a)(iii) or 209.3(1)(a)(iv) • The live-in caregiver wasn't living in a private home in Canada…

pageTitle: Employer-specific work permits
heading: Employer-specific work permits
text: There are 2 types of work permits: employer-specific work permits and open work permits . To be eligible for an employer-specific work permit, you need to meet certain requirements. Also, your Canadian employer needs to take steps before you can apply.

pageTitle: Employer-specific work permits
heading: What is an employer-specific work permit
text: An employer-specific work permit lets you work in Canada under the conditions of the permit until the expiry date . The details are listed on your permit under the following sections: • Additional Information – includes the employer, location and occupation • Conditions – consists of a numbered list of requirements you need to meet • Remarks – may contain additional clarifications or conditions that you may need to meet See an example of a work permit . You must comply with all the information listed on your work permit. • International Experience Canada (Young Professionals category) • Global Skills Strategy • all employer-specific work permits with special instructions

pageTitle: Employer-specific work permits
heading: What you need to apply
text: To apply for an employer-specific work permit, you need • to have a job offer from an employer who’s not on the list of non-compliant employers • to meet the general requirements for all work permit applicants Some people don’t need a work permit to work in Canada. If you need a work permit and don’t yet have one, you can’t • legally work in Canada or • write on a job application that you’re legally allowed to work in Canada Search jobs for temporary foreign workers on the Job Bank .

pageTitle: Employer-specific work permits
heading: Ask your employer about a labour market impact assessment
text: A labour market impact assessment (LMIA) is a document that proves there’s a need to hire a foreign worker to fill the job. Most jobs need an LMIA. Before you apply for a work permit, your employer needs to • find out if they need an LMIA for your job or if it’s LMIA-exempt • apply for an LMIA (if needed) • give you documents, like an LMIA letter and Annex A from their positive LMIA, which you’ll need to include in your application Find out if you need an LMIA to hire a temporary foreign worker .

pageTitle: Employer-specific work permits
heading: Is this the right work permit for you
text: Answer a few questions to find out • if an employer-specific work permit is right for you • if you need to follow special instructions to apply Find out which type of work permit you need.

pageTitle: Employer-specific work permits
heading: Apply for an employer-specific work permit
text: The instructions to apply for a work permit vary depending on where you’re applying. Apply from outside Canada Apply at a port of entry Extend your work permit

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions on your work permit
heading: Processing time
text: We couldn’t load the data. Try again If you have a work permit that’s about to expire or that you need to change, you must apply to extend it or change the conditions on it. You should apply to extend your work permit at least 30 days before your current permit expires.

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
text: • What to do if your work permit expired • Changing jobs or employers • If you want to travel outside Canada If you have an employer-specific work permit and you want to change jobs or employers in Canada, you must apply for a new work permit , if you’re eligible. You may be able to start your new job or work for a new employer before your work permit application is approved. You can request authorization to work while we process your application. Open work permit holders can change employers at any time while their work permit is valid.

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: Who’s eligible
text: To be eligible, you must meet the following conditions: • You applied for a new employer-specific work permit in Canada . • You currently either have an employer-specific work permit , or • are a worker who can work without a work permit ( not including business visitors or short-term workers under the Global Skills Strategy ) If you have lost the job connected to your employer-specific work permit and would like to continue working in Canada, you must • get a new job offer and apply to renew your work permit , while remaining in Canada • stop working for this, or any other, employer until your new work permit is approved If you apply to change jobs or employers before your current work permit expires, you might be able to keep working with a work authorization . If you can’t get a new job offer, you need to leave Canada by the “ must leave by ” date printed on your work permit.

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: How to request authorization to work while we process your application
text: International Experience Canada (IEC) participants must first check that they can change employers with an IEC work permit . To change jobs or employers, follow these steps after you apply for your new employer-specific work permit : Use the IRCC web form to let us know you need to change jobs or employers. • Select See details under the Other category and then Continue to form , when prompted. Under the Your enquiry section of the IRCC web form , copy the text below that applies to your situation. Make sure you fill out the sections in square brackets, which include the • name(s) of employer(s) If you applied on paper and don’t know your application number, include your courier tracking number. This will allow us to find your application. If you have other questions, you need to fill out an IRCC web form . You must include the code PPCHANGEWORK2020 when you copy and paste the text below. We can’t prioritize your request without this code. Priority Code PPCHANGEWORK2020: I am requesting consideration under the Temporary public policy to exempt foreign nationals in Canada from certain requirements when changing employment and ask that the applicable exemptions be granted until a decision is made on my work permit application or it is withdrawn. I attest that I hold/held a work permit valid until [DATE] for employer [ name ] / in occupation [NOC code ] and I have received a new job offer for [new employer name ] / [new occupation NOC code ]. I have submitted my application for a work permit online or I submitted my paper application and the postal/courier tracking number is [ number ]. I intend to work for the [new employer name ] / [new occupation NOC code ] as specified in the aforementioned work permit. I understand that providing false, misleading or incorrect…

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: Problem with the online application
text: If you’re trying to apply for a work permit online in one of the categories below, you may have problems getting the right result. If you’re not sure if this applies to you, ask your employer. • Permanent residence applicants in Canada who used Express Entry to submit their permanent residence application, and • have received an acknowledgment of receipt letter (A75) • Open work permit pilot program for permanent residence applicants in the spouse or common-law partner in Canada class (A70) • Foreign physicians coming to work in Quebec (C10) • Refugee claimants (S61) • Failed refugee claimants waiting for removal who need to support themselves (S62) • Persons under an unenforceable removal order (S62) • Live-in caregivers who have submitted their permanent residence application (A71) • In-Canada applicants under the Home Child-Care Provider Pilot or the Home Support Worker Pilot (C90) and their family members (C91) • Post-graduation work permit extension (C43) • Destitute students (H81) • Holders of a temporary resident permit that’s valid for a minimum of 6 months (H82) • Start-up visa workers (A75) • International Experience Canada: Extend your participation or change conditions on your work permit (C21) • Seafood-processing workers • Agricultural workers who are not part of the Seasonal Agricultural Worker Program • Visitors in Canada applying for an initial study permit, work permit or both who are accompanying a spouse holding a low-skilled work permit

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
text: • Who can extend Who can extend • After you extend After you extend • What to do if your work permit expired What to do if your work permit expired • Changing jobs or employers Changing jobs or employers • If you want to travel outside Canada If you want to travel outside Canada The Canada Post labour disruption will cause delays in mail delivery We’re still processing applications normally. However, there will be delays in • sending applications mailed within or to Canada We encourage you to apply online if possible . If you need to apply on paper, use another courier to send us your application.

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: Maintain your status
text: If you apply to extend or change the conditions of your work permit before it expires, you’re legally allowed to stay in Canada while we process your application. If your work permit expires while we process your application, find out if you can keep working . Temporary resident permit (TRP) applicants can’t maintain temporary resident status while their application is being processed . This applies to both • initial TRP applications, and • any other TRP applications To maintain your status , we must receive your application before your work permit expires. We determine the date and time an application is received 2 ways: • If you apply online: You must submit your application before midnight Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) on the day your work permit expires. Our online application system uses UTC. • Make sure you know the difference between your local time and UTC. • If you apply on paper: We stamp paper applications the day we receive them at the case processing centre. If we receive an application after your work permit expires, we’ll count back 7 days and use that as the date received.

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: Before you extend or change the conditions of your work permit
text: Before you apply, make sure your employer has completed all the required steps . Some jobs require an LMIA. Your employer is responsible for submitting the application for an LMIA. Don’t apply for a work permit until your employer gives you a positive LMIA decision letter. Check the processing times for LMIA applications.

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: How to extend or change the conditions of your work permit
text: In most cases, you must apply online to extend or change the conditions of your work permit. • You can make sure your application is complete before you submit it. • You’ll get a letter (work permit extension [WP-EXT] except post-graduation work permit [PGWP]) that proves you can keep working under the same conditions as your current work permit . You won’t get this letter if you apply on paper, because we won’t know if your application is complete until we review it. • There are no courier fees or mail delivery time — we get your application instantly. • You can quickly submit more documents online (if we ask you to). • You get updates on the status of your application directly in your online account. • As of June 21, 2024, you can no longer apply for a PGWP at a port of entry (airport, land or marine border) when entering Canada. You should apply at least 30 days before your current permit expires. However, if you apply too early, the validity of your existing work permit may be cut short by getting your new work permit before the current one ends. If you applied to extend your work permit and then your permit expires, you have • maintained your status , and • authorization to work without a permit until we make a decision on your application You can keep working under the same conditions of your current work permit. If you have an employer-specific work permit, this means having the same Open work permit holders can still change employers and jobs. While you’re on maintained status , you won’t be able to renew documents issued by your province or territory, such as your Make sure your documents are valid before your work permit expires. While on maintained status (while your work permit is expired), you can’t apply for a SIN card or update the expiry date of your…

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: Priority processing for work permit applicants
text: We’re prioritizing certain work permit applications. You may be eligible for faster processing for an employer-specific work permit if you • have a full-time and non-seasonal job offer in one of the three eligible NOCs NOC 31100 – Specialist in clinical laboratory medicine • NOC 31101 – Specialists in surgery • NOC 31102 – General practitioners and family physicians • have a support letter from a Canadian province or territory The letter nominates you as a permanent residence applicant through the Provincial Nominee Program. • meet the general eligibility requirements (opens in a new tab) to apply for a work permit, depending on where you apply from Get faster processing We’re prioritizing the applications of people who perform or support essential services . When you fill out the work permit application form To get priority processing, follow these steps: • Get the National Occupational Classification (NOC) number your employer entered on the offer of employment or • labour market impact assessment (LMIA) • Enter that number in the Job title box. This is box 4 in the Details of intended work in Canada section. • You must enter only the number . On November 16, 2022, we switched to the 2021 version of the NOC system. If you apply for a work permit on or after November 16, 2022, make sure you use the NOC 2021 codes . • Butchers – retail and wholesale (NOC 63201) • Meat cutters and fishmongers – retail and wholesale (NOC 65202) • Agricultural service contractors and farm supervisors (NOC 82030) • Livestock labourers (NOC 85100) • Specialized livestock workers and farm machinery operators (NOC 84120) • Harvesting labourers (NOC 85101) • Nursery and greenhouse labourers (NOC 85103) • Fish and seafood plant workers (NOC 94142) • Labourers in food and beverage processing…

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: Problem with the online application
text: If you’re trying to apply for a work permit online in one of the categories below, you may have problems getting the right result. If you’re not sure if this applies to you, ask your employer. • Permanent residence applicants in Canada who used Express Entry to submit their permanent residence application, and • have received an acknowledgment of receipt letter (A75) • Open work permit pilot program for permanent residence applicants in the spouse or common-law partner in Canada class (A70) • Foreign physicians coming to work in Quebec (C10) • Refugee claimants (S61) • Failed refugee claimants waiting for removal who need to support themselves (S62) • Persons under an unenforceable removal order (S62) • Live-in caregivers who have submitted their permanent residence application (A71) • In-Canada applicants under the Home Child-Care Provider Pilot or the Home Support Worker Pilot (C90) and their family members (C91) • Post-graduation work permit extension (C43) • Destitute students (H81) • Holders of a temporary resident permit that’s valid for a minimum of 6 months (H82) • Start-up visa workers (A75) • International Experience Canada: Extend your participation or change conditions on your work permit (C21) • Seafood-processing workers • Agricultural workers who are not part of the Seasonal Agricultural Worker Program • Visitors in Canada applying for an initial study permit, work permit or both who are accompanying a spouse holding a low-skilled work permit

pageTitle: Extend or change the conditions of your work permit
heading: What to do if you want to travel outside Canada and then re-enter
text: A work permit isn’t a travel document. It doesn’t give you the right to travel to Canada. Along with your permit, you need a valid passport and either • an electronic travel authorization (eTA) Having an eTA or a visa, along with your work permit, doesn’t guarantee that you can re-enter Canada. You must show the border services officer that you meet all the requirements before you can re-enter. Along with your work permit, you need: • an eTA (if you plan to travel to Canada by air) You don’t need an eTA if you’re arriving by car, bus, train or boat (including cruise ship). Note: When we approved your initial work permit, we automatically issued an eTA to you. See your letter of introduction for details. If we issue your renewed work permit on or after May 1, 2017 , we will automatically issue you an eTA as well. If we issued your renewed work permit before May 1, 2017 and you don’t already have an eTA, you will need to get one before you return to Canada by air. As of April 26, 2022, lawful permanent residents of the United States must show these documents for all methods of travel to Canada: • a valid passport from their country of nationality (or an equivalent acceptable travel document) and • a valid green card (or equivalent valid proof of status in the United States) You need an official proof of status as a lawful permanent resident of the U.S., such as one of the following: • valid permanent resident card (Form I-551) • foreign passport with an unexpired temporary I-551 stamp (also known as an Alien Documentation, Identification and Telecommunication [ADIT] stamp) • foreign passport with a temporary I-551 printed notation (“Upon endorsement serves as temporary I-551 evidencing permanent residence for 1 year”) on a machine-readable immigrant visa upon endorsement…

pageTitle: Hire a home care worker (caregiver)
heading: Hire a home care worker (caregiver)
text: There are a few ways you can hire a home care worker (caregiver). Your options depend on • whether you’re inside or outside Quebec • where the home care worker (caregiver) will work The information on this page applies to employers looking to hire a • home child care provider ( National Occupation Classification 44100 ) • home support worker ( National Occupation Classification 44101 ) If you need to hire a worker in another occupation , follow the process to hire a foreign worker .

pageTitle: Hire a home care worker (caregiver)
heading: Hire a worker who’s in Canada
text: If you’re an employer (outside Quebec), you can hire a home care worker through one of the programs below. The Home Care Worker Immigration pilots : Workers in Canada stream allows Canadian employers (outside Quebec) to hire qualified home care workers already in Canada. How to hire under this pilot Closed to new applicants on June 17, 2024 You can only hire workers through these pilots if either • the worker already has an open work permit without restrictions that would prevent them from working in child care or health services • the worker already has a work permit under one of the pilots You can only hire workers through this program if they meet these 2 conditions: • the worker either already has a work or study permit • is allowed to work in Canada without a work permit (this doesn’t apply to business visitors ) • you can get a positive Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA) and have your candidate apply for a new LMIA-based work permit Hire through the Temporary Foreign Worker You can only hire workers under this program if • the worker already has an open work permit without restrictions that would prevent them from working in child care or health services

pageTitle: Hire a home care worker (caregiver)
heading: Hire a worker who’s outside Canada
text: If you’re an employer (outside Quebec), you can hire a home care worker through one of the programs below. How to hire under this pilot (once it opens) Closed to new applicants on June 17, 2024 You can only hire workers through these pilots if either • you already submitted a job offer to a worker, and they submitted their permanent residence application before the program closed • the worker already has an open work permit without restrictions that would prevent them from working in child care or health services You can only hire through this program if the worker will work in Quebec. Hire through the Temporary Foreign Worker Program .

pageTitle: Hire a home care worker (caregiver)
heading: Hire a worker as an employer in Quebec
text: If you’re an employer in Quebec, you can hire a home care worker who’s outside or in Canada through the Temporary Foreign Worker Program .

pageTitle: Find out if you need a labour market impact assessment
heading: Find out if you need a labour market impact assessment
text: Hire a temporary foreign worker A labour market impact assessment (LMIA) determines if the hiring of a temporary foreign worker will have a positive or negative effect on Canada’s labour market. A positive LMIA confirms that • no Canadians or permanent residents are available to do the job • there is a need for a temporary foreign worker Most employers need an LMIA before they can hire a temporary foreign worker.

pageTitle: Find out if you need a labour market impact assessment
heading: Determine if you need an LMIA
text: Before you hire the worker, you must determine if • the worker needs a work permit • Review the LMIA exemption codes to determine if you need an LMIA • work permit exemptions to determine if the worker needs a work permit • Select the LMIA exemption code or work permit exemption that seems most relevant to your situation. • Read the detailed descriptions to determine if the exemption applies to you. If an LMIA exemption applies • you can hire without an LMIA • the worker needs a work permit • you must include the LMIA exemption code in your offer of employment If a work permit exemption applies • the worker doesn’t need a work permit, but • they must check if they need a visa to come to Canada If an exemption doesn’t apply, then • you need an LMIA to hire the worker and • the worker needs a work permit

pageTitle: Find out if you need a labour market impact assessment
heading: How to hire if you need an LMIA
text: If you determine that you need an LMIA, you’ll need to hire through the Temporary Foreign Worker Program. Employment and Social Development Canada • processes LMIA applications • issues the decision letter If you receive a positive LMIA decision letter, you need to take both of these steps: • Provide each worker with a copy of the decision letter • the letter’s Annex A – Employment details • Tell them to apply for a work permit . Learn more about hiring with an LMIA.

pageTitle: Find out if you need a labour market impact assessment
heading: How to hire if you don’t need an LMIA
text: You can hire without an LMIA through the International Mobility Program. Learn more about hiring without an LMIA.

pageTitle: Find out if you need a labour market impact assessment
heading: Hiring in Quebec
text: You’ll need to meet different requirements depending on whether you’re hiring with or without an LMIA. • get a Quebec Acceptance Certificate (CAQ) from the Ministère de l’Immigration, de la Francisation et de l’Intégration (MIFI) • give a copy of the CAQ to your worker When the worker applies for a work permit, they must submit • a copy of the positive LMIA decision letter Find out how to hire with an LMIA in Quebec. The worker doesn’t need a CAQ if you’re hiring through the International Mobility Program. Learn more about hiring without an LMIA.

pageTitle: Find out if you need a labour market impact assessment
heading: Hiring top talent with faster processing
text: The Global Skills Strategy (GSS) allows you to hire highly skilled workers and get the application processed faster. Depending on the job, you can hire through the GSS with or without an LMIA . Find out how to hire through the GSS.

pageTitle: Hire a permanent foreign worker
heading: Hire a permanent foreign worker
text: Hire foreign workers and recent graduates to fill labour or skills shortages. Support an employee in their journey to permanent residence. Are you looking to hire a temporary worker? Learn about your hiring options. Are you looking to hire a temporary worker? Learn about your hiring options .

pageTitle: Hire a permanent foreign worker
heading: Find a skilled worker
text: Hire a new worker from abroad or support an employee in getting permanent residence How to work with a province or territory to nominate a skilled worker Become a designated employer and hire a skilled worker or international graduate Become a designated employer in a participating community and hire a candidate Options for employers hiring a foreign worker as a home care worker (caregiver)

pageTitle: Hire a permanent foreign worker
heading: Features
text: Read the guide to find out how to hire and retain internationally trained workers for your business

pageTitle: Hire a newcomer as an intern
heading: Hire a newcomer as an intern
text: The Federal Internship for Newcomers (FIN) Program helps public service managers hire eligible newcomers to fill temporary job opportunities. The FIN Program helps you fill skilled labour shortages by hiring newcomers if you’re • an employer in a federal government department or agency • government organization • located in or around Fredericton You can hire candidates in fields such as • procurement and material management New These skilled and experienced newcomers can enhance your workplace by • increasing productivity and creativity • bringing new perspectives • helping to expand local and global networks All qualified candidates have • been assessed by a partner immigrant-serving organization • good language skills and are ready to work • taken job–readiness training (if needed) • been interviewed and evaluated by Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Once interns begin their placement, we match them with mentors and offer training sessions to help them adapt to working in Canada. You can support your interns by allowing them to attend these professional development sessions whenever possible. Many internships start in September, but you can request to hire an intern throughout the year.

pageTitle: Hire a newcomer as an intern
heading: Hiring process
text: • Send us the statement of criteria by email or fill out and submit our online request form . • We’ll match candidates to your needs and send you their profiles via VidCruiter within five business days. • You’ll receive an instruction email to set up a hiring manager’s account on VidCruiter. This is easy – it will only take you 2 minutes. • You’ll then review qualified candidates' profiles on VidCruiter, conduct informal interviews and check references. • Lastly, you’ll complete the steps to prepare a letter of offer and initiate the security clearance process. • Pay your hired candidate or candidates their salary. According to the Public Service Employment Act , permanent residents and Canadians have equal preference in publicly available job postings.

pageTitle: Hire a newcomer as an intern
heading: Support
text: If you're interested in hiring a FIN intern, you can receive: • a resource guide with information on steps to follow throughout the duration of the internship • tip sheets to help managers build strong, diverse teams • mentoring and mentorship opportunities available to managers and interns • support and resources available to you

pageTitle: Work and Travel in Canada with International Experience Canada
heading: Follow:
text: If you're 18 to 35 (18 to 30 in some countries), come work and travel in Canada through International Experience Canada (IEC)! It's a government program designed to help you • gain valuable international work experience • improve your language skills in English and French • work in Canada and fund the trip of a lifetime Are you Canadian? Learn how you can work and travel abroad with IEC. Are you Canadian? Learn how you can work and travel abroad with IEC .

pageTitle: Find out what type of work permit you need
heading: Find out what type of work permit you need
text: Get the right instructions to apply. Answer a few questions to find out: • if you need a work permit or if you’re exempt • if you need an open or employer-specific work permit • if you need special instructions to apply Find out what type of work permit you need

pageTitle: Open work permits
heading: Open work permits
text: There are 2 types of work permits: open work permits and employer-specific work permits . You can get an open work permit in certain situations.

pageTitle: Open work permits
heading: What is an open work permit
text: An open work permit lets you work for any employer in Canada, except those who • are on the non-compliant list , or • regularly offer striptease, erotic dance or escort services, or erotic massages You don’t need a job offer to apply for an open work permit. Open work permits aren’t job-specific, so if you have one, your employer doesn’t need to • apply for a labour market impact assessment • submit an offer of employment • pay the employer compliance fee

pageTitle: Open work permits
heading: Restrictions on your open work permit
text: You may have restrictions listed on your open work permit related to the following: • what type of work you can do Example: You may need a medical exam to work in some jobs. • Find out how to remove the medical conditions from your work permit . • where you can work Example: If you have an open work permit under the provincial nominee class, you may be able to work only in a specific province. • Find out if you can work at a different location from the one listed on your open work permit .

pageTitle: Open work permits
heading: Is this the right work permit for you
text: You need to follow special instructions if you’re applying for certain open work permits. Answer a few questions to find out • if you’re eligible for an open work permit • if you need to follow special instructions to apply Find out which type of work permit you need.

pageTitle: Open work permits
heading: Apply for an open work permit
text: The instructions to apply for a work permit vary depending on where you’re applying. Apply from outside Canada Apply at a port of entry Extend your work permit

pageTitle: Work permit: Applying from inside Canada
heading: Work permit: Applying from inside Canada
text: Check if you can apply from outside Canada or at a port of entry (POE) . Most foreign nationals need a work permit to work in Canada. You may be eligible for an employer-specific or open work permit.

pageTitle: Work permit: Applying from outside Canada
heading: Work permit: Applying from outside Canada
text: Check if you can apply from inside Canada or at a port of entry (POE) . Most foreign nationals need a work permit to work in Canada. You may be eligible for an employer-specific or an open work permit.

pageTitle: Work permit: Applying at a port of entry (POE)
heading: Work permit: Applying at a port of entry (POE)
text: Not applying at a POE? Check if you can apply from outside Canada or at a inside Canada . Most foreign nationals need a work permit to work in Canada. You may be eligible for an employer-specific or open work permit. Get the instructions to apply for a work permit at a port of entry.

pageTitle: Work permits for permanent residence applicants
heading: Work permits for permanent residence applicants
text: Apply for a work permit that lets you or your family members work while you wait for a decision on your permanent residence.

pageTitle: Work permits for permanent residence applicants
heading: Services and information
text: For permanent residence applicants (including Express Entry, provincial nominees) with an expiring work permit Open work permit for a sponsored spouse or common-law partner and accompanying children Open work permit to meet the 12-month work experience requirement in the Gaining experience category Open work permits for you and your family members 2-year work permit to work for the employer who offered you the job 2-year open work permit to start setting up your business while you wait for a decision Work permits for Quebec Selection Certificate (CSQ) holders 1-year work permit to work for the employer who offered you the job in the participating community 2-year work permit to work for the employer who offered you the job in the participating community 2-year work permit to work for the employer who offered you the job in the participating community Open work permits for your family depending on your TEER category Location-specific work permit for Yukon nominees under the provincial nominee program. 3-year open work permit to work while you wait for a decision Faster processing for your employer-specific work permit Answer a few questions to see different ways you might be able to come to Canada

pageTitle: Work permits with special instructions

pageTitle: Work permits with special instructions

pageTitle: Work permits with special instructions

pageTitle: Work permits with special instructions
heading: Open work permits

pageTitle: Work permits with special instructions
